STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_39.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 39: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[39]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_39.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""M-60 (2026-04-23): V30 explicit gap reporting + manifest.
2	
3	V30 Report Contract Architecture Layer 4c. Codex plan review #4:
4	  "add machine-readable metadata for every incomplete slot:
5	   slot_id, entity_id, status, failure_reason, retrieval_attempt_log,
6	   available_artifacts, human_completion_eligible. The prose gap
7	   paragraph is appropriate for report.md, but it is not enough
8	   by itself."
9	
10	M-60 has two responsibilities:
11	  1. Compose the structured `frame_coverage_report` block that
12	     ships in manifest.json — consumed by M-61 human-completion
13	     task generation + by downstream build gates + by the
14	     operator dashboard.
15	  2. Compose a Methods-section snippet for report.md disclosing
16	     the frame_gap_count so clinicians can see at a glance how
17	     many contract slots weren't fully populated.
18	
19	## What M-60 does NOT do
20	
21	- Does NOT own the per-slot rendered prose. M-58 render_slot_prose
22	  handles that; M-58 ships the GAP_PROSE_MARKER constant; M-59
23	  validates the marker is present. M-60 consumes M-59's
24	  ValidationReport to decide which slots need coverage entries.
25	- Does NOT write to disk. The manifest composer returns a dict;
26	  the caller (sweep assembly) decides whether to write it.
27	
28	## Pure function
29	
30	Same inputs → byte-identical coverage report. No wall-clock, no
31	network. `retrieval_attempt_log` passthrough preserves M-56's
32	already-deterministic attempt log (payload-deterministic via
33	M-56 pass-2 Blocker-1 fix).
34	
35	## Interaction with other layers
36	
37	  M-55 CompiledFrame         → entity bindings + research_question
38	  M-56 FrameRows             → provenance_class + retrieval_attempts
39	                               + failure_reason + available artifacts
40	  M-57 ContractOutline       → slot membership + sections
41	  M-58 SlotFillPayload       → completion_count, not used here
42	                               directly but M-59 feeds per-slot
43	                               verdicts to M-60
44	  M-59 ValidationReport      → per-entity verdicts that become the
45	                               `status` field in coverage entries
46	  M-60 FrameCoverageReport   → structured manifest block (this)
47	  M-61 human_gap_tasks.json  → consumes
48	                               `human_completion_eligible=True`
49	                               entries
50	"""
51	from __future__ import annotations
52	
53	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
54	from typing import Any
55	
56	from ..nodes.contract_outline import ContractOutline
57	from ..nodes.frame_compiler import CompiledFrame
58	from ..retrieval.frame_fetcher import FrameRow, ProvenanceClass
59	from .slot_validator import ValidationReport, ValidationVerdict
60	
61	
62	@dataclass(frozen=True)
63	class SlotCoverageEntry:
64	    """One entry per contract slot in the manifest.
65	
66	    Codex plan review #4 required all seven fields below. Extras
67	    (section, subsection_title, doi, pmid, required_fields,
68	    entity_type) are carried so M-61 task generation and operator
69	    dashboards don't have to re-join against the contract.
70	
71	    `is_pipeline_fault` flags the defensive path where M-56 did
72	    not emit a FrameRow for a contracted entity (pipeline
73	    crossed wires). Codex M-60 audit Medium: these entries should
74	    NOT be routed to human completion — they need engineer
75	    attention, not curator attention.
76	
77	    `human_curated_provenance` surfaces the M-61 StructuredProvenance
78	    dict (curator_id, artifact_sha256, retention path, attestation,
79	    etc.) directly in the manifest entry when the row was supplied
80	    via Path B. Codex M-61 audit Blocker 3 — audit evidence must
81	    survive the FrameRow → manifest boundary, not live in an
82	    in-memory side channel.
83	    """
84	
85	    slot_id: str
86	    entity_id: str
87	    entity_type: str
88	    section: str
89	    subsection_title: str
90	    status: str                       # from ValidationVerdict enum value
91	    provenance_class: str
92	    failure_reason: str | None
93	    retrieval_attempt_log: list[dict[str, Any]]
94	    available_artifacts: list[str]
95	    human_completion_eligible: bool
96	    is_pipeline_fault: bool
97	    # Contract echoes for M-61 consumption (Codex M-60 audit Blocker)
98	    required_fields: list[str]
99	    min_fields_for_completion: int
100	    doi: str | None
101	    pmid: str | None
102	    # M-61 audit evidence passthrough
103	    human_curated_provenance: dict[str, str] | None = None
104	
105	
106	@dataclass(frozen=True)
107	class FrameCoverageReport:
108	    """Top-level manifest block. Composed by M-60; serialized as
109	    manifest["frame_coverage_report"] at sweep assembly time."""
110	
111	    research_question: str
112	    schema_version: str
113	    total_slots: int
114	    total_entities: int
115	    frame_gap_count: int
116	    partial_count: int
117	    pass_count: int
118	    # Codex M-60 audit Medium: distinguish pipeline faults (M-56
119	    # did not emit a FrameRow for a contracted entity — engineer
120	    # attention needed) from retrieval gaps (curator/human
121	    # completion needed).
122	    pipeline_fault_count: int
123	    by_status: dict[str, int]
124	    entries: tuple[SlotCoverageEntry, ...]
125	
126	    def to_manifest_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
127	        """JSON-serializable dict shape for manifest.json write.
128	
129	        Uses `dataclasses.asdict` on entries then converts the
130	        tuple to list so the result is a plain Python dict tree
131	        that json.dump can handle without a custom encoder.
132	        """
133	        return {
134	            "research_question": self.research_question,
135	            "schema_version": self.schema_version,
136	            "total_slots": self.total_slots,
137	            "total_entities": self.total_entities,
138	            "frame_gap_count": self.frame_gap_count,
139	            "partial_count": self.partial_count,
140	            "pass_count": self.pass_count,
141	            "pipeline_fault_count": self.pipeline_fault_count,
142	            "by_status": dict(self.by_status),
143	            "entries": [asdict(e) for e in self.entries],
144	        }
145	
146	
147	def compose_frame_coverage(
148	    compiled_frame: CompiledFrame,
149	    outline: ContractOutline,
150	    frame_rows: tuple[FrameRow, ...],
151	    validation_report: ValidationReport,
152	    strict_verify_by_key: dict[Any, Any] | None = None,
153	) -> FrameCoverageReport:
154	    """M-60 entrypoint. Produce the structured coverage block for
155	    manifest.json.
156	
157	    Args:
158	        compiled_frame: M-55 CompiledFrame (research_question,
159	            schema_version, evidence_bindings).
160	        outline: M-57 ContractOutline (slot membership + sections).
161	        frame_rows: M-56 FrameRows parallel to compiled_frame.evidence_bindings.
162	        validation_report: M-59 ValidationReport with per-entity
163	            verdicts.
164	
165	    Returns:
166	        FrameCoverageReport — one SlotCoverageEntry per (slot, entity)
167	        combination in outline order, with aggregate counts.
168	
169	    Deterministic: iteration follows outline order; frame_rows are
170	    keyed by entity_id; validation_report entities read in order.
171	    Same inputs → byte-identical output.
172	    """
173	    rows_by_eid: dict[str, FrameRow] = {
174	        r.entity_id: r for r in frame_rows
175	    }
176	    # Pull contract for per-entity required_fields lookup (Codex
177	    # M-60 audit Blocker — M-61 task payload needs missing-field
178	    # detail).
179	    contract_entities = compiled_frame.contract.entities_by_id()
180	    # Build verdicts keyed by (slot_id, entity_id) for O(1) lookup
181	    verdicts_by_key: dict[tuple[str, str], str] = {}
182	    reasons_by_key: dict[tuple[str, str], str] = {}
183	    for ev in validation_report.entity_validations:
184	        verdicts_by_key[(ev.slot_id, ev.entity_id)] = ev.verdict.value
185	        reasons_by_key[(ev.slot_id, ev.entity_id)] = ev.reason
186	
187	    entries: list[SlotCoverageEntry] = []
188	    gap_count = 0
189	    partial_count = 0
190	    pass_count = 0
191	    pipeline_fault_count = 0
192	    by_status: dict[str, int] = {}
193	
194	    for section in outline.sections:
195	        for slot in section.slots:
196	            for entity_id in slot.entity_ids:
197	                row = rows_by_eid.get(entity_id)
198	                status = verdicts_by_key.get(
199	                    (slot.slot_id, entity_id),
200	                    ValidationVerdict.FAIL_MISSING_PAYLOAD.value,
201	                )
202	                by_status[status] = by_status.get(status, 0) + 1
203	                contract_entity = contract_entities.get(entity_id)
204	                required_fields = (
205	                    list(contract_entity.required_fields)
206	                    if contract_entity else []
207	                )
208	                min_fields = (
209	                    contract_entity.min_fields_for_completion
210	                    if contract_entity else 0
211	                )
212	
213	                if row is None:
214	                    # M-56 should have produced a row; defensive
215	                    # fallback. Codex M-60 audit Medium: this is a
216	                    # pipeline-integrity fault, not a retrieval
217	                    # gap. NOT routed to human completion.
218	                    entries.append(_empty_coverage_entry(
219	                        slot_id=slot.slot_id,
220	                        entity_id=entity_id,
221	                        entity_type=(
222	                            contract_entity.type
223	                            if contract_entity else "unknown"
224	                        ),
225	                        section=section.section,
226	                        subsection_title=slot.subsection_title,
227	                        status=status,
228	                        validator_reason=reasons_by_key.get(
229	                            (slot.slot_id, entity_id), ""
230	                        ),
231	                        required_fields=required_fields,
232	                        min_fields_for_completion=min_fields,
233	                    ))
234	                    pipeline_fault_count += 1
235	                    continue
236	
237	                is_gap_row = (
238	                    row.provenance_class
239	                    == ProvenanceClass.FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE
240	                )
241	
242	                # I-ready-017 FX-07b leg-2 (#1111) honesty override (root-cause
243	                # design, Codex-ratified). A non-gap entity that VALIDATED
244	                # (verdict==pass) but contributes ZERO SUBSTANTIVE verified prose
245	                # to the report MUST NOT read as pass. Owner routing matters:
246	                #   - generator HAD a usable quote AND drafted substantive prose
247	                #     that strict_verify then dropped to zero -> generation_failed
248	                #     (pipeline fault; engineer-owned; not human-completable).
249	                #   - otherwise (no usable quote, OR only not-extractable
250	                #     placeholders drafted) -> curator_gap_no_substantive_content
251	                #     (a curator can supply licensed full-text; human-completable;
252	                #     NOT a pipeline fault — mis-routing it to engineers would be
253	                #     a different dishonesty).
254	                # The discriminator is TOKEN-INDEPENDENT (sentences_kept_substantive
255	                # / sentences_drafted_substantive from contract_section_runner), so
256	                # it catches the Class-A (metadata_only/empty-quote: dropped
257	                # disclosures carry no [#ev:] token) and Class-B (placeholder-kept)
258	                # escapes that a token-counted metric missed. Triple-guarded:
259	                # extraction/retrieval gaps (verdict!=pass), FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE
260	                # rows, and missing telemetry (default-None) are NEVER reclassified
261	                # (byte-identical when telemetry is absent).
262	                _sv_meta = (strict_verify_by_key or {}).get(
263	                    (slot.slot_id, entity_id)
264	                )
265	                _meta_ok = isinstance(_sv_meta, dict)
266	                _zero_substantive = bool(
267	                    (not is_gap_row)
268	                    and status == ValidationVerdict.PASS.value
269	                    and _meta_ok
270	                    and (_sv_meta.get("sentences_kept_substantive") or 0) == 0
271	                )
272	                _is_gen_failed = bool(
273	                    _zero_substantive
274	                    and bool(_sv_meta.get("has_usable_quote"))
275	                    and (_sv_meta.get("sentences_drafted_substantive") or 0) > 0
276	                )
277	                _is_curator_gap = bool(_zero_substantive and not _is_gen_failed)
278	                _override_status = (
279	                    "generation_failed" if _is_gen_failed
280	                    else "curator_gap_no_substantive_content" if _is_curator_gap
281	                    else status
282	                )
283	                if _is_gen_failed:
284	                    # Would-be pass with no verified prose, but the generator had
285	                    # real source — engineer-owned pipeline fault.
286	                    pipeline_fault_count += 1
287	                    by_status[status] = max(0, by_status.get(status, 0) - 1)
288	                    by_status["generation_failed"] = (
289	                        by_status.get("generation_failed", 0) + 1
290	                    )
291	                elif _is_curator_gap:
292	                    # Would-be pass with no substantive verified prose and no
293	                    # usable source — a curator-fixable coverage gap. Rolled up
294	                    # with frame gaps for the clinician summary; by_status keeps
295	                    # the precise status. NOT a pass, NOT a pipeline fault.
296	                    gap_count += 1
297	                    by_status[status] = max(0, by_status.get(status, 0) - 1)
298	                    by_status["curator_gap_no_substantive_content"] = (
299	                        by_status.get(
300	                            "curator_gap_no_substantive_content", 0
301	                        ) + 1
302	                    )
303	                elif is_gap_row:
304	                    gap_count += 1
305	                elif status == ValidationVerdict.PASS.value:
306	                    pass_count += 1
307	                else:
308	                    # Non-gap row that failed validation counts as
309	                    # partial coverage (content exists but didn't
310	                    # meet min_fields or lacks citation).
311	                    partial_count += 1
312	
313	                entries.append(SlotCoverageEntry(
314	                    slot_id=slot.slot_id,
315	                    entity_id=entity_id,
316	                    entity_type=row.entity_type,
317	                    section=section.section,
318	                    subsection_title=slot.subsection_title,
319	                    status=_override_status,
320	                    provenance_class=row.provenance_class.value,
321	                    failure_reason=(
322	                        "strict_verify dropped all generated content sentences "
323	                        "(pipeline fault — no verified prose for this entity)"
324	                        if _is_gen_failed
325	                        else (
326	                            "no substantive verified prose — only "
327	                            "not-extractable disclosures; curator must supply a "
328	                            "licensed copy covering the required fields"
329	                        )
330	                        if _is_curator_gap
331	                        else _failure_reason(
332	                            row, status, reasons_by_key.get(
333	                                (slot.slot_id, entity_id), ""
334	                            ),
335	                        )
336	                    ),
337	                    retrieval_attempt_log=[
338	                        {
339	                            "source": a.source,
340	                            "url": a.url,
341	                            "attempt_index": a.attempt_index,
342	                            "http_status": a.http_status,
343	                            "outcome": a.outcome,
344	                        }
345	                        for a in row.retrieval_attempts
346	                    ],
347	                    available_artifacts=_available_artifacts(row),
348	                    # Codex M-60 audit pass-2 blocker: eligibility is
349	                    # strictly "curator can fix". Engineer-owned
350	                    # statuses (unbound citation, gap-no-language,
351	                    # payload mismatch) and already-passing entries
352	                    # are NOT routed to human completion.
353	                    human_completion_eligible=(
354	                        False if _is_gen_failed
355	                        # Use the EFFECTIVE (post-override) status so the
356	                        # curator_gap_no_substantive_content outcome routes to a
357	                        # human task (it is in the _is_curator_actionable
358	                        # allowlist); generation_failed already short-circuits
359	                        # False above.
360	                        else _is_curator_actionable(
361	                            is_gap_row=is_gap_row, status=_override_status,
362	                        )
363	                    ),
364	                    is_pipeline_fault=_is_gen_failed,
365	                    required_fields=required_fields,
366	                    min_fields_for_completion=min_fields,
367	                    doi=row.doi,
368	                    pmid=row.pmid,
369	                    # M-61 audit evidence passthrough (Codex M-61
370	                    # audit Blocker 3 fix). None for non-human-
371	                    # curated rows.
372	                    human_curated_provenance=row.human_curated_provenance,
373	                ))
374	
375	    return FrameCoverageReport(
376	        research_question=compiled_frame.research_question,
377	        schema_version=compiled_frame.schema_version,
378	        total_slots=sum(
379	            len(s.slots) for s in outline.sections
380	        ),
381	        total_entities=len(entries),
382	        frame_gap_count=gap_count,
383	        partial_count=partial_count,
384	        pass_count=pass_count,
385	        pipeline_fault_count=pipeline_fault_count,
386	        by_status=by_status,
387	        entries=tuple(entries),
388	    )
389	
390	
391	def compose_methods_disclosure(
392	    coverage: FrameCoverageReport,
393	) -> str:
394	    """Compose the Methods-section snippet disclosing frame gap
395	    count. Attached to report.md so clinicians see at a glance
396	    how many contract slots weren't fully populated.
397	
398	    Deterministic prose; no LLM. Three shapes:
399	      - all-pass (zero partial, zero gap, zero pipeline-fault)
400	      - partial-only (some partial, no gap, no pipeline-fault)
401	      - gaps-present (at least one retrieval gap)
402	      - pipeline-fault (surfaces separately; Codex M-60 Medium)
403	    """
404	    # FX-07 (I-ready-017) leg 1: a slot can be status=PASS yet its evidence is
405	    # only abstract_only / metadata_only (NOT full text). Those count toward
406	    # pass_count, so the old "all N populated with bound evidence" footer
407	    # contradicted the body ("did not survive strict verification" / abstract-
408	    # only). Surface shallow-provenance pass entries as a disclosed gap; only
409	    # claim "all bound" when every pass entry is full-text (open_access).
410	    _SHALLOW_PROVENANCE = {
411	        ProvenanceClass.ABSTRACT_ONLY.value,
412	        ProvenanceClass.METADATA_ONLY.value,
413	    }
414	    shallow_entries = [
415	        e for e in coverage.entries
416	        if e.status == ValidationVerdict.PASS.value
417	        and e.provenance_class in _SHALLOW_PROVENANCE
418	    ]
419	    shallow_count = len(shallow_entries)
420	    fully_bound_count = coverage.pass_count - shallow_count
421	
422	    has_issues = (
423	        coverage.frame_gap_count
424	        or coverage.partial_count
425	        or coverage.pipeline_fault_count
426	        or shallow_count
427	    )
428	    if not has_issues:
429	        return (
430	            f"Frame coverage: all {coverage.total_entities} "
431	            f"contract-required entities populated with bound evidence."
432	        )
433	
434	    lines = [
435	        "Frame coverage disclosure (V30 Report Contract):",
436	        (
437	            f"  - Total contract-required entities: "
438	            f"{coverage.total_entities}"
439	        ),
440	        f"  - Fully populated (full-text bound evidence): {fully_bound_count}",
441	    ]
442	    if shallow_count:
443	        shallow_names = ", ".join(
444	            sorted(e.entity_id for e in shallow_entries)
445	        )
446	        lines.append(
447	            f"  - Populated from abstract/metadata only (full text NOT "
448	            f"retrieved): {shallow_count} ({shallow_names})"
449	        )
450	    if coverage.partial_count:
451	        lines.append(
452	            f"  - Partial coverage (below min_fields or unbound "
453	            f"citation): {coverage.partial_count}"
454	        )
455	    if coverage.frame_gap_count:
456	        lines.append(
457	            f"  - Unretrievable (paywalled with no OA/abstract): "
458	            f"{coverage.frame_gap_count}"
459	        )
460	    if coverage.pipeline_fault_count:
461	        # Codex M-60 audit Medium: distinct line item. A pipeline
462	        # fault is NOT an unretrievable paywalled gap — it's an
463	        # engineering bug (M-56 failed to produce a row for a
464	        # contracted entity). Surface it explicitly so readers
465	        # don't mistake it for an expected retrieval failure.
466	        lines.append(
467	            f"  - Pipeline faults (engineer investigation "
468	            f"required): {coverage.pipeline_fault_count}"
469	        )
470	    lines.append(
471	        "  - Gap slots render explicit gap language in the "
472	        "relevant subsection; see manifest.json "
473	        "frame_coverage_report for per-slot detail."
474	    )
475	    return "\n".join(lines)
476	
477	
478	def compose_human_completion_tasks(
479	    coverage: FrameCoverageReport,
480	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
481	    """Compose M-61 hybrid-completion task list (Path B).
482	
483	    Codex plan review #6 + M-60 audit Blocker: M-61 must receive
484	    per-entity doi/pmid/required_fields/failure_reason. Each
485	    task dict now carries the full contract's required_fields
486	    list plus a failure-specific `needs` string telling the
487	    operator what the curator must deliver for this entry.
488	
489	    M-60 provides the upstream source here; M-61's own layer
490	    threads provenance attestation (artifact hash, retention,
491	    curator_id) when the operator fulfills the task.
492	
493	    Returns JSON-serializable list of task dicts; caller writes
494	    to human_gap_tasks.json or equivalent.
495	
496	    Pipeline-fault entries (is_pipeline_fault=True) are NOT
497	    included — those need engineer attention, not curator
498	    attention.
499	    """
500	    tasks: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
501	    for entry in coverage.entries:
502	        if not entry.human_completion_eligible:
503	            continue
504	        if entry.is_pipeline_fault:
505	            # Defensive: human_completion_eligible should already
506	            # be False for pipeline faults, but guard explicitly.
507	            continue
508	        tasks.append({
509	            "entity_id": entry.entity_id,
510	            "entity_type": entry.entity_type,
511	            "slot_id": entry.slot_id,
512	            "section": entry.section,
513	            "subsection_title": entry.subsection_title,
514	            "doi": entry.doi,
515	            "pmid": entry.pmid,
516	            "failure_reason": entry.failure_reason,
517	            "status": entry.status,
518	            "retrieval_attempt_log": entry.retrieval_attempt_log,
519	            "available_artifacts": entry.available_artifacts,
520	            # Codex M-60 audit Blocker: M-61 task payload must
521	            # carry required_fields so the operator knows what to
522	            # deliver. Also include min_fields_for_completion so
523	            # the curator sees the success threshold.
524	            "required_fields": list(entry.required_fields),
525	            "min_fields_for_completion": entry.min_fields_for_completion,
526	            "needs": _compose_task_needs(entry),
527	        })
528	    return tasks
529	
530	
531	def _compose_task_needs(entry: SlotCoverageEntry) -> str:
532	    """Failure-specific operator guidance.
533	
534	    Codex M-60 audit pass-2 realignment: this function is ONLY
535	    called for curator-actionable entries (engineer-owned statuses
536	    and already-passing entries are filtered upstream by
537	    `_is_curator_actionable`). So only two branches matter:
538	
539	      - gap row: RETRIEVAL guidance (curator provides licensed
540	        content to fill the primary-publication gap).
541	      - non-gap row with fail_min_fields: EXTRACTION guidance
542	        (curator provides richer licensed copy to cover fields
543	        that M-56's abstract / metadata couldn't supply).
544	
545	    Any other status reaching here is a routing bug — emit a
546	    defensive catch-all so it shows up in manifest rather than
547	    being silently invisible.
548	    """
549	    required_csv = ", ".join(entry.required_fields) or "(none declared)"
550	    if entry.provenance_class == "frame_gap_unrecoverable":
551	        return (
552	            f"RETRIEVAL gap: source not reachable via open-access, "
553	            f"abstract, or metadata paths. Operator must provide "
554	            f"direct_quote from a licensed copy covering at least "
555	            f"{entry.min_fields_for_completion} of "
556	            f"[{required_csv}], with structured provenance "
557	            f"(artifact hash, retention pointer, curator_id)."
558	        )
559	    if entry.status == "fail_min_fields":
560	        return (
561	            f"EXTRACTION gap: retrieval succeeded but only a "
562	            f"subset of required fields was extractable from the "
563	            f"available content. Operator must supplement with "
564	            f"a direct_quote from a richer licensed copy covering "
565	            f"the missing fields in [{required_csv}]."
566	        )
567	    if entry.status == "curator_gap_no_substantive_content":
568	        # I-ready-017 FX-07b leg-2 (#1111): validated row whose generated prose
569	        # was zero substantive verified sentences (only not-extractable
570	        # disclosures / no usable quote). Curator supplies a licensed copy.
571	        return (
572	            f"SUBSTANTIVE-CONTENT gap: the entity validated but no substantive "
573	            f"verified prose could be produced from the available content "
574	            f"(only not-extractable disclosures). Operator must provide a "
575	            f"direct_quote from a licensed copy covering at least "
576	            f"{entry.min_fields_for_completion} of [{required_csv}], with "
577	            f"structured provenance (artifact hash, retention pointer, "
578	            f"curator_id)."
579	        )
580	    # Defensive catch-all. Should not fire if _is_curator_actionable
581	    # is kept in sync with this function. Surface the routing drift
582	    # loudly in the manifest rather than silently mislabeling it.
583	    return (
584	        f"ROUTING CHECK: entry status={entry.status!r} reached "
585	        f"the curator task composer but does not match a known "
586	        f"curator-actionable case. Verify "
587	        f"_is_curator_actionable / _compose_task_needs alignment."
588	    )
589	
590	
591	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
592	# Helpers
593	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
594	def _is_curator_actionable(is_gap_row: bool, status: str) -> bool:
595	    """Codex M-60 audit pass-2 + pass-3: strict allowlist of
596	    curator-actionable (status, row-type) combinations.
597	
598	    Pass-3 residual: pass-2 used a denylist that defaulted unknown
599	    statuses to True. A future verdict added without updating this
600	    predicate would silently route to curator. Pass-3 switches to
601	    an allowlist so new statuses default to engineer routing.
602	
603	    Curator-actionable:
604	      (gap,     FAIL_MIN_FIELDS)      — gap row, curator can
605	                                        supply licensed content
606	      (gap,     FAIL_MISSING_PAYLOAD) — gap row, M-58 never ran
607	                                        or couldn't run
608	      (non-gap, FAIL_MIN_FIELDS)      — retrieval worked but
609	                                        extraction fell short;
610	                                        curator supplies richer copy
611	
612	    NOT curator-actionable (engineer or no-op):
613	      (any,     PASS)                 — nothing to do
614	      (any,     FAIL_UNBOUND_CITATION) — M-58 render wiring
615	      (any,     FAIL_GAP_NO_LANGUAGE)  — M-58 gap-template
616	      (any,     FAIL_PAYLOAD_MISMATCH) — pipeline crossed wires
617	      (non-gap, FAIL_MISSING_PAYLOAD)  — non-gap without payload
618	                                        usually means M-58
619	                                        didn't run; engineer
620	                                        attention more likely
621	                                        than curator content
622	
623	    Any (row_type, status) not in the allowlist defaults to
624	    engineer routing. New verdicts MUST be explicitly added.
625	    """
626	    curator_actionable = {
627	        (True,  ValidationVerdict.FAIL_MIN_FIELDS.value),
628	        (True,  ValidationVerdict.FAIL_MISSING_PAYLOAD.value),
629	        (False, ValidationVerdict.FAIL_MIN_FIELDS.value),
630	        # I-ready-017 FX-07b leg-2 (#1111): the honesty override's curator-gap
631	        # outcome — a non-gap entity that read pass but produced zero substantive
632	        # verified prose with no usable source. A curator supplies licensed
633	        # full-text; NOT a pipeline fault.
634	        (False, "curator_gap_no_substantive_content"),
635	    }
636	    return (is_gap_row, status) in curator_actionable
637	
638	
639	def _available_artifacts(row: FrameRow) -> list[str]:
640	    """Deterministic ordered list of artifact kinds M-56 produced
641	    for this entity. M-61 operator dashboard shows this so the
642	    curator sees what's already available (e.g. "oa_pdf_url" means
643	    the operator can fetch full-text; "metadata_only" means they
644	    need to supply everything). For HUMAN_CURATED rows, adds
645	    "human_curated_provenance" so auditors see the structured
646	    evidence is attached."""
647	    out: list[str] = []
648	    if row.oa_pdf_url:
649	        out.append("oa_url")
650	    if row.direct_quote:
651	        out.append(row.quote_source)
652	    if row.title:
653	        out.append("crossref_metadata")
654	    if row.doi:
655	        out.append("doi")
656	    if row.pmid:
657	        out.append("pmid")
658	    if row.url:
659	        out.append("url_pattern")
660	    if row.human_curated_provenance is not None:
661	        out.append("human_curated_provenance")
662	    return out
663	
664	
665	def _failure_reason(
666	    row: FrameRow, status: str, validator_reason: str,
667	) -> str | None:
668	    """Compose a single failure_reason string per manifest
669	    consumers' expectations. Gap rows carry M-56's failure_reason;
670	    partial/unbound rows carry M-59's validator reason.
671	
672	    The pipeline-fault path is handled by `_empty_coverage_entry`,
673	    not by this function.
674	    """
675	    if row.provenance_class == ProvenanceClass.FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE:
676	        return row.failure_reason
677	    if status == ValidationVerdict.PASS.value:
678	        return None
679	    return validator_reason or "unknown"
680	
681	
682	def _empty_coverage_entry(
683	    slot_id: str,
684	    entity_id: str,
685	    entity_type: str,
686	    section: str,
687	    subsection_title: str,
688	    status: str,
689	    validator_reason: str,
690	    required_fields: list[str],
691	    min_fields_for_completion: int,
692	) -> SlotCoverageEntry:
693	    """Fallback for the (should-not-happen) case where a FrameRow
694	    is missing for a contracted entity.
695	
696	    Codex M-60 audit Medium fix: this is a pipeline-integrity
697	    fault, not a retrieval gap. Pipeline-fault message dominates
698	    validator wording so engineers see the root cause first. NOT
699	    routed to human completion — a curator cannot fix a pipeline
700	    bug.
701	    """
702	    # Prepend the pipeline-fault diagnosis so it leads. Append
703	    # validator wording only if it adds specificity.
704	    pipeline_msg = (
705	        "FrameRow missing for contracted entity — M-56 did not "
706	        "produce a row (pipeline crossed wires; engineer "
707	        "attention required)"
708	    )
709	    failure_reason = (
710	        f"{pipeline_msg}; validator also noted: {validator_reason}"
711	        if validator_reason
712	        else pipeline_msg
713	    )
714	    return SlotCoverageEntry(
715	        slot_id=slot_id,
716	        entity_id=entity_id,
717	        entity_type=entity_type,
718	        section=section,
719	        subsection_title=subsection_title,
720	        status=status,
721	        provenance_class="pipeline_fault",
722	        failure_reason=failure_reason,
723	        retrieval_attempt_log=[],
724	        available_artifacts=[],
725	        # Codex M-60 audit Medium: do NOT route pipeline faults to
726	        # human completion. A curator cannot reconcile a missing
727	        # M-56 row; this needs engineer intervention.
728	        human_completion_eligible=False,
729	        is_pipeline_fault=True,
730	        required_fields=required_fields,
731	        min_fields_for_completion=min_fields_for_completion,
732	        doi=None,
733	        pmid=None,
734	        human_curated_provenance=None,
735	    )
736

1	"""Generation-level resume checkpoint: persist the generated section DRAFTS so a
2	``--resume`` can SKIP the expensive multi-section generation and re-enter at the
3	post-generation faithfulness gates.
4	
5	WHY (SPEED, FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL):
6	    Today the latest resume checkpoint is ``corpus_snapshot.json`` (POST-SELECTION),
7	    so every ``--resume`` re-runs the ~30-40min multi-section generation before it can
8	    re-reach the verification/4-role stages. After a VERIFICATION bug-fix the operator
9	    pays that full generation cost again just to exercise the changed gate. This module
10	    persists the generator's per-section RAW DRAFTS (plus the accepted outline) at the
11	    single post-generation seam, so a resume reloads the drafts, skips the section-draft
12	    LLM calls, and re-enters the SAME strict_verify / NLI-repair / 4-role / D8 path.
13	
14	WHAT THE SNAPSHOT PERSISTS (DATA ONLY):
15	    * the accepted OUTLINE: a flat list of ``SectionPlan`` dataclasses (title / focus /
16	      ev_ids / archetype) — ``asdict`` / ``SectionPlan(**d)`` round-trip, no live object.
17	      The outline is itself an LLM call (``_call_outline``) on a fresh run, so it MUST be
18	      persisted or the reloaded draft keys would not line up with a re-derived structure.
19	    * the per-section RAW DRAFT prose, keyed by section title. ``raw_draft`` is the
20	      generator's draft text BEFORE provenance-token rewrite + strict_verify (the candidate
21	      sentences the gate inspects). It is the ONLY input that lets strict_verify re-run
22	      MEANINGFULLY on resume — ``verified_text`` is already post-drop, so re-verifying it
23	      could never catch a strict_verify bug (the wrongly kept/dropped sentences are baked in).
24	    * ITEM 5a (I-arch-007 #1264 — LOSSLESS verification re-entry): the verdict-FREE
25	      per-section ``SectionPlan`` view (section id / title / atom-id list) AND the per-section
26	      atom/claim CATALOG (atom_id -> evidence-id / span bindings). The post-generation
27	      verification stage consumes per-section ``SectionPlan`` metadata + the per-section
28	      atom/claim catalog (which atoms/claims belong to each section + their evidence bindings),
29	      NOT just the raw section text. ``raw_drafts`` ALONE cannot deterministically re-enter
30	      strict_verify/NLI/D8 the way a fresh run does, so this metadata is persisted as DATA so a
31	      resume re-enters LOSSLESSLY. The SWEEP owner (ITEM 5 in run_honest_sweep_r3.py) consumes
32	      these. Every atom binding is PRE-verdict DATA (atom_id/evidence_id/span_start/span_end/
33	      literal_text) — NO ``is_verified``, NO ``release_outcome``; it remains subject to the
34	      ``_FORBIDDEN_VERDICT_KEYS`` guard, validated RECURSIVELY over the nested structure.
35	    * a ``generation_flags`` slate: the env flags that change WHAT the generator produces
36	      (distill / atom-refusal mode / outline mode). A cached raw draft is a faithful
37	      strict_verify input ONLY under the same flag config that produced it; on resume the
38	      loader FAILS LOUD if the active flags differ.
39	
40	HARD INVARIANT (CLAUDE.md §-1.3, ABSOLUTE — mirrors corpus_snapshot.py):
41	    A checkpoint stores DATA, NEVER A VERDICT.
42	    The snapshot stores ONLY the outline (plan DATA) + raw draft PROSE + the verdict-free
43	    section/atom metadata (ITEM 5a) + the flag slate. It stores NO strict_verify result, NO
44	    NLI/4-role/D8 decision, NO "verified" flag, NO release outcome — at ANY nesting depth. On
45	    ``--resume`` the caller reloads this DATA and the generator RE-RUNS strict_verify / the
46	    NLI-driven sentence-repair loop on every reloaded draft, then the downstream 4-role / D8
47	    seam RE-RUNS from scratch — exactly as a fresh run does. Only the section-draft LLM call is
48	    skipped; every faithfulness gate still fully executes. This module makes a relaxation
49	    structurally impossible: it never serializes a verdict, and ``load_generation_snapshot``
50	    fail-loud-rejects a payload that smuggled one at ANY depth (the RECURSIVE guard,
51	    Codex P2-3).
52	
53	PRECISE "SKIP GENERATION" CONTRACT:
54	    "skip generation" means "skip the initial section-draft LLM calls". strict_verify can
55	    still trigger a per-section REGENERATION (an LLM call) when the reloaded draft fails the
56	    kept-fraction floor — that regen runs exactly as on a fresh run. A resume is therefore
57	    cheap, not necessarily zero-LLM-cost.
58	
59	The snapshot is a plain JSON document (schema_version pinned) so it is human-auditable.
60	No pickle, no code execution on load.
61	"""
62	
63	from __future__ import annotations
64	
65	import json
66	import os
67	from dataclasses import asdict, is_dataclass
68	from pathlib import Path
69	from typing import Any
70	
71	# Bump on any incompatible schema change. A reload that sees a different version FAILS LOUD
72	# (refuses to resume) rather than silently feeding a stale-shaped draft set to the verifier.
73	# v2 (ITEM 5a, I-arch-007 #1264): added the verdict-free per-section ``section_plans`` view +
74	# per-section ``section_atom_catalogs`` for LOSSLESS verification re-entry. A v1 payload (no
75	# section metadata) is REFUSED — a resume from it could not re-enter strict_verify/NLI/D8
76	# losslessly, so failing loud is correct (re-run fresh).
77	GENERATION_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 2
78	
79	# The canonical snapshot filename inside a per-query run_dir.
80	GENERATION_SNAPSHOT_FILENAME = "generation_snapshot.json"
81	
82	# Stage pointer. Only ``post_generation`` is persisted today (the seam right after the
83	# generator produces section drafts, before the post-generation verification/4-role gates).
84	STAGE_POST_GENERATION = "post_generation"
85	
86	# The master env flag gating the generation-snapshot feature (LAW VI: no hard-coded behavior).
87	# Default ON: a fresh run WRITES the snapshot; a ``--resume`` PREFERS it over corpus_snapshot
88	# when present. Set to a falsey value to disable both the write and the resume-load.
89	GENERATION_SNAPSHOT_ENV = "PG_GENERATION_SNAPSHOT"
90	
91	# The env flag whose mode, when not "off", makes a cached-draft resume DIVERGE from a fresh run:
92	# the post-hoc atom-refusal validator consumes the per-section ``atom_catalog`` that ONLY the live
93	# ``_call_section`` produces. A reloaded-draft section has no live catalog (the cached path sets it
94	# empty), so under ``log_only``/``strict`` the validator would mark every reloaded section
95	# "skipped_empty_catalog" / degraded — a fresh run with a real catalog certifies them. That is a
96	# resume != fresh DIVERGENCE (a spurious extra-strictness, not a relaxation), so a generation-stage
97	# resume is REFUSED outright whenever this mode is active (``assert_atom_refusal_mode_resumable``).
98	ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE_ENV = "PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE"
99	ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE_OFF = "off"
100	
101	# Generation-affecting env flags whose values are captured into the snapshot slate. A cached
102	# raw draft is a faithful strict_verify input ONLY under the same values; on resume the loader
103	# FAILS LOUD on any mismatch (see ``assert_generation_flags_match``). These are the flags that
104	# change WHAT the generator emits as a draft (NOT verification-only flags, which are SUPPOSED to
105	# differ on a verification-fix resume — that is the entire point of the feature).
106	GENERATION_AFFECTING_ENV_FLAGS: tuple[str, ...] = (
107	    # PG_SECTION_DISTILL changes the section-writing path (REDUCE over a distilled ledger vs
108	    # legacy quote-blocks) => a draft produced under one is not a faithful input to the other.
109	    "PG_SECTION_DISTILL",
110	    # PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE: in non-"off" modes the post-hoc atom validator consumes the
111	    # per-section atom_catalog that ONLY the live _call_section produces. A reloaded-draft
112	    # section has no live catalog, so a non-"off" mode cannot be faithfully replayed and the
113	    # loader refuses the resume rather than silently shipping a degraded section.
114	    "PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE",
115	    # PG_GENERATOR_MODEL pins the writer; a draft produced by a different writer is a different
116	    # candidate set (recorded for audit + mismatch fail-loud).
117	    "PG_GENERATOR_MODEL",
118	    # PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN gates the credibility pass that annotates the drafted
119	    # sentences' disclosure; recorded so a resume under a different setting is rejected.
120	    "PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN",
121	)
122	
123	# Verdict tokens a DATA-ONLY checkpoint may NEVER contain (mirrors the A12 forbidden set in
124	# run_honest_sweep_r3.py). If any appears ANYWHERE in a payload (top-level OR nested in the
125	# section/atom metadata), a verdict leaked in and the loader/saver FAILS LOUD — a silent load
126	# would relax the only hard gate (§-1.3 ABSOLUTE). Validated RECURSIVELY (Codex P2-3).
127	_FORBIDDEN_VERDICT_KEYS = frozenset(
128	    {
129	        "release_outcome",
130	        "release_allowed",
131	        "released",
132	        "verified",
133	        "is_verified",
134	        "verified_text",
135	        "final_verdicts",
136	        "d8_decision",
137	        "four_role_evaluation",
138	        "strict_verify_result",
139	    }
140	)
141	
142	
143	class GenerationSnapshotError(RuntimeError):
144	    """Raised when a --resume reload cannot trust the on-disk generation snapshot.
145	
146	    FAIL LOUD (LAW II): a missing/corrupt/version-mismatched/verdict-leaked/flag-mismatched
147	    snapshot must NOT silently fall back to a fresh generation under --resume (the operator
148	    asked to skip generation; a silent re-bill would mask the interruption and defeat the
149	    feature). The caller surfaces this as a clean fail-loud — never a silent fresh run.
150	    """
151	
152	
153	def _assert_no_verdict_keys_recursive(obj: Any, *, path: str = "<root>") -> None:
154	    """RECURSIVELY fail-loud if a forbidden verdict key appears at ANY nesting depth.
155	
156	    Codex P2-3 (I-arch-007 #1264): the ITEM-5a section/atom metadata is NESTED
157	    (section -> atom_catalog -> atom dict), so a top-level-only guard would let a verdict
158	    key leak through a nested structure. This walks every dict key + every list element so
159	    NO ``is_verified`` / ``release_outcome`` / ``verified_text`` (etc.) can survive at any
160	    depth — on BOTH the save path (refuse to PERSIST a leaked verdict) and the load path
161	    (refuse to LOAD one). The walk is data-only (dict/list/scalars); a JSON payload has no
162	    other container types, so this is total over a parsed snapshot.
163	    """
164	    if isinstance(obj, dict):
165	        leaked = sorted(_FORBIDDEN_VERDICT_KEYS & set(obj.keys()))
166	        if leaked:
167	            raise GenerationSnapshotError(
168	                f"generation snapshot at {path} contains FORBIDDEN verdict key(s) {leaked} — "
169	                "a checkpoint stores DATA ONLY (§-1.3); a resume re-runs every gate and can NEVER "
170	                "replay a stored decision. Refusing (recursive verdict-key guard, Codex P2-3)."
171	            )
172	        for key, value in obj.items():
173	            _assert_no_verdict_keys_recursive(value, path=f"{path}.{key}")
174	    elif isinstance(obj, (list, tuple)):
175	        for index, value in enumerate(obj):
176	            _assert_no_verdict_keys_recursive(value, path=f"{path}[{index}]")
177	
178	
179	def generation_snapshot_path(run_dir: Path) -> Path:
180	    """Deterministic generation-snapshot location for a per-query run_dir."""

193	    """Snapshot the current values of the generation-affecting env flags (DATA, no verdict).

201	def _outline_payload(outline: list[Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
202	    """Serialize the accepted outline (list of SectionPlan dataclasses) to flat dicts.
203	
204	    Each ``SectionPlan`` is a flat dataclass (title / focus / ev_ids / archetype) so ``asdict``
205	    is a safe DATA-only round-trip. A non-dataclass entry is a programming error and fails loud
206	    (no silent drop) so the persisted outline can never diverge from the live one.
207	    """
208	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
209	    for plan in outline or []:
210	        if not is_dataclass(plan):
211	            raise GenerationSnapshotError(
212	                f"generation snapshot: outline entry {plan!r} is not a SectionPlan dataclass; "
213	                "refusing to persist a non-DATA outline element"
214	            )
215	        rows.append(asdict(plan))
216	    return rows

219	def _section_plans_payload(section_plans: dict[str, Any] | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
220	    """ITEM 5a: serialize the verdict-FREE per-section SectionPlan view.
221	
222	    Maps section_id -> {title, atom_ids}. This is the minimal plan-DATA the verification
223	    re-entry needs to line each reloaded draft up with its atoms (NOT a verdict). ``atom_ids``
224	    is the ordered list of atom-id strings the section drew from; ``title`` is the section
225	    heading. DATA-only: no verified flag, no release outcome (the recursive guard enforces it).
226	    A None/empty input serializes to ``{}`` (a fresh run that produced no atom-bearing sections,
227	    e.g. OFF mode — the loader treats empty as "no metadata to re-enter with").
228	    """
229	    out: dict[str, Any] = {}
230	    for section_id, view in (section_plans or {}).items():
231	        if not isinstance(view, dict):
232	            raise GenerationSnapshotError(
233	                f"generation snapshot: section_plans[{section_id!r}] is {view!r}, not a dict; "
234	                "refusing to persist a non-DATA section-plan view"
235	            )
236	        atom_ids = view.get("atom_ids", [])
237	        out[str(section_id)] = {
238	            "title": str(view.get("title", "")),
239	            "atom_ids": [str(a) for a in (atom_ids or [])],
240	        }
241	    return out

244	# The DATA-only fields of a ClaimAtom that bind an atom to its evidence span. The atom/claim
245	# catalog persists EXACTLY these (atom_id -> evidence-id / span bindings) — never a verdict.
246	# Mirrors src.polaris_graph.generator.claim_atom_extractor.ClaimAtom identity+provenance fields.
247	_ATOM_BINDING_FIELDS: tuple[str, ...] = (
248	    "atom_id",
249	    "evidence_id",
250	    "span_start",
251	    "span_end",
252	    "literal_text",
253	)

256	def _atom_binding(atom: Any, *, section_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
257	    """Extract the DATA-only atom->evidence-id/span binding from one catalog entry.
258	
259	    Accepts either a ClaimAtom dataclass (the live ``atom_catalog`` value type) or a plain
260	    dict (already-serialized). Persists ONLY the identity+provenance binding fields — never a
261	    verdict. A missing required binding field fails loud (no silent partial binding).
262	    """
263	    if is_dataclass(atom):
264	        source = asdict(atom)
265	    elif isinstance(atom, dict):
266	        source = atom
267	    else:
268	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
269	            f"generation snapshot: section_atom_catalogs[{section_id!r}] entry {atom!r} is "
270	            "neither a ClaimAtom dataclass nor a dict; refusing to persist a non-DATA atom"
271	        )
272	    binding: dict[str, Any] = {}
273	    for field_name in _ATOM_BINDING_FIELDS:
274	        if field_name not in source:
275	            raise GenerationSnapshotError(
276	                f"generation snapshot: section_atom_catalogs[{section_id!r}] atom is missing the "
277	                f"required binding field {field_name!r}; refusing to persist an incomplete atom "
278	                "binding (a resume would mis-bind the claim to its evidence span)"
279	            )
280	        binding[field_name] = source[field_name]
281	    return binding

284	def _dict_atoms_only(section_atom_catalogs: dict[str, Any] | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
285	    """Return a view of the catalogs with frozen-ClaimAtom values EXCLUDED, for the input guard.
286	
287	    A frozen ``ClaimAtom`` dataclass is verdict-free DATA by construction (its fields are the
288	    fixed identity/provenance/semantic set — no verdict field exists), and the recursive guard
289	    walks dict/list/scalars (not arbitrary dataclasses). So the raw-input verdict-key guard only
290	    needs to inspect DICT/LIST caller-supplied atom entries — those are the only ones that could
291	    smuggle a verdict key. This drops dataclass atoms from the walked view (their bindings are
292	    still extracted verbatim by ``_atom_binding``); dict atoms are kept so the guard sees them.
293	    """
294	    out: dict[str, Any] = {}
295	    for section_id, catalog in (section_atom_catalogs or {}).items():
296	        if isinstance(catalog, dict):
297	            entries = list(catalog.values())
298	        elif isinstance(catalog, (list, tuple)):
299	            entries = list(catalog)
300	        else:
301	            # A malformed catalog type — keep it so _section_atom_catalogs_payload fails loud on it.
302	            out[str(section_id)] = catalog
303	            continue
304	        out[str(section_id)] = [a for a in entries if not is_dataclass(a)]
305	    return out
306

308	def _section_atom_catalogs_payload(
309	    section_atom_catalogs: dict[str, Any] | None,
310	) -> dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]:
311	    """ITEM 5a: serialize the per-section atom/claim catalog as DATA-only span bindings.
312	
313	    Maps section_id -> [ {atom_id, evidence_id, span_start, span_end, literal_text}, ... ].
314	    The live ``atom_catalog`` is a ``dict[atom_id, ClaimAtom]``; we persist its values as the
315	    ordered list of evidence-span bindings the verification re-entry needs. Verdict-FREE; the
316	    recursive guard re-asserts it. A None/empty input serializes to ``{}``.
317	    """
318	    out: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = {}
319	    for section_id, catalog in (section_atom_catalogs or {}).items():
320	        sid = str(section_id)
321	        bindings: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
322	        if isinstance(catalog, dict):
323	            atoms = catalog.values()
324	        elif isinstance(catalog, (list, tuple)):
325	            atoms = catalog
326	        else:
327	            raise GenerationSnapshotError(
328	                f"generation snapshot: section_atom_catalogs[{sid!r}] is {catalog!r}, neither a "
329	                "dict[atom_id, ClaimAtom] nor a list; refusing to persist a non-DATA catalog"
330	            )
331	        for atom in atoms:
332	            bindings.append(_atom_binding(atom, section_id=sid))
333	        out[sid] = bindings
334	    return out

337	def save_generation_snapshot(
338	    run_dir: Path,
339	    *,
340	    run_id: str,
341	    question: str,
342	    slug: str,
343	    domain: str,
344	    outline: list[Any],
345	    section_raw_drafts: dict[str, str],
346	    had_contract_sections: bool,
347	    section_plans: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
348	    section_atom_catalogs: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
349	    stage: str = STAGE_POST_GENERATION,
350	) -> Path:
351	    """Persist the post-generation snapshot. Returns the written path.
352	
353	    Atomic write (temp + os.replace) so a kill DURING the snapshot write never leaves a
354	    truncated/half-parsed file that a later --resume would choke on.
355	
356	    ``outline`` is the ACCEPTED SectionPlan list (post outline-call / research_plan / contract
357	    injection). ``section_raw_drafts`` maps section title -> the generator's RAW DRAFT prose
358	    (pre strict_verify). ``had_contract_sections`` records whether the run used V30 contract
359	    plans: a reloaded plain-SectionPlan outline cannot reconstruct a ContractSectionPlanExt, so
360	    a resume of such a run must FAIL LOUD rather than silently route contract sections through
361	    the legacy path (a behavior divergence vs the fresh run).
362	
363	    ITEM 5a (I-arch-007 #1264): ``section_plans`` is the verdict-FREE per-section view
364	    (section_id -> {title, atom_ids}); ``section_atom_catalogs`` is the per-section atom/claim
365	    catalog (section_id -> dict[atom_id, ClaimAtom] | list[ClaimAtom]). Both are persisted as
366	    DATA-only span bindings so a resume re-enters the verification stage LOSSLESSLY. The whole
367	    payload is validated RECURSIVELY for leaked verdict keys BEFORE the write (Codex P2-3) — a
368	    leaked ``is_verified`` / ``release_outcome`` at ANY depth fails loud rather than persisting.
369	
370	    The payload carries DATA ONLY — outline + draft prose + verdict-free section/atom metadata +
371	    the flag slate. It carries NO verdict; a resume re-runs every faithfulness gate on the
372	    reloaded drafts.
373	    """
374	    run_dir = Path(run_dir)
375	    # Codex P2-3: fail loud on a leaked verdict key in the RAW caller-supplied section/atom
376	    # metadata BEFORE the binding-field whitelist (_atom_binding / _section_plans_payload) could
377	    # silently STRIP it. Whitelisting alone would drop a smuggled ``is_verified`` instead of
378	    # refusing — that is a silent strip, not the fail-loud §-1.3 demands. Guarding the raw input
379	    # (dataclass values are excluded — a frozen ClaimAtom is by construction verdict-free DATA;
380	    # only dict/list caller input can smuggle a key) makes a leaked decision impossible to persist.
381	    _assert_no_verdict_keys_recursive(
382	        {"section_plans": section_plans or {}}, path="<input>.section_plans"
383	    )
384	    _assert_no_verdict_keys_recursive(
385	        _dict_atoms_only(section_atom_catalogs), path="<input>.section_atom_catalogs"
386	    )
387	    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
388	        "schema_version": GENERATION_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION,
389	        "stage": stage,
390	        "run_id": run_id,
391	        "question": question,
392	        "slug": slug,
393	        "domain": domain,
394	        # DATA: the accepted outline (plan structure the drafts are keyed against).
395	        "outline": _outline_payload(outline),
396	        # DRAFT PROSE ONLY (re-verified on resume) — never a verified flag.
397	        "section_raw_drafts": {str(k): str(v or "") for k, v in (section_raw_drafts or {}).items()},
398	        # ITEM 5a: verdict-FREE per-section SectionPlan view (id -> title + atom-id list).
399	        "section_plans": _section_plans_payload(section_plans),
400	        # ITEM 5a: per-section atom/claim catalog (atom -> evidence-id/span bindings, DATA-only).
401	        "section_atom_catalogs": _section_atom_catalogs_payload(section_atom_catalogs),
402	        # The generation-affecting flag slate: a cached draft is faithful ONLY under these values.
403	        "generation_flags": _capture_generation_flags(),
404	        # Whether the run used V30 contract sections (resume of such a run fails loud).
405	        "had_contract_sections": bool(had_contract_sections),
406	        # EXPLICIT invariant marker so a §-1.1 auditor sees the contract on the artifact itself.
407	        "faithfulness_invariant": (
408	            "DATA ONLY; outline + raw section drafts + verdict-free section/atom metadata + flag "
409	            "slate; NO verdict/verified_text stored at any depth. A resume re-runs strict_verify + "
410	            "NLI-repair + 4-role + D8 on every reloaded draft; only the section-draft LLM call is "
411	            "skipped."
412	        ),
413	    }
414	    # Codex P2-3: refuse to PERSIST a payload that smuggled a verdict key at ANY nesting depth
415	    # (the ITEM-5a section/atom metadata is nested). Fail loud BEFORE the write so a leaked
416	    # decision can never reach disk.
417	    _assert_no_verdict_keys_recursive(payload)
418	    path = generation_snapshot_path(run_dir)
419	    tmp = path.with_suffix(path.suffix + ".tmp")
420	    tmp.write_text(
421	        json.dumps(payload, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
422	        encoding="utf-8",
423	    )
424	    os.replace(tmp, path)
425	    return path
426

428	def load_generation_snapshot(run_dir: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
429	    """Reload + validate the generation snapshot for --resume. Returns the parsed payload.
430	
431	    Raises ``GenerationSnapshotError`` on absent / malformed / version-mismatched / verdict-
432	    leaked / empty-draft snapshots so the caller fails loud instead of resuming on bad data.
433	    Returns DATA ONLY — the caller MUST re-run every faithfulness gate on the reloaded drafts.
434	    Flag-slate compatibility is a SEPARATE check (``assert_generation_flags_match``) so the
435	    caller can surface a precise "active flags differ from the snapshot" message.
436	    """
437	    path = generation_snapshot_path(run_dir)
438	    if not path.exists():
439	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
440	            f"--resume: no generation snapshot at {path} (nothing to resume at the generation "
441	            f"stage; the earlier corpus_snapshot path handles a pre-generation resume)"
442	        )
443	    try:
444	        payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
445	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
446	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
447	            f"--resume: generation snapshot at {path} is unreadable/corrupt: {exc}"
448	        ) from exc
449	    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
450	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
451	            f"--resume: generation snapshot at {path} is not a JSON object"
452	        )
453	    version = payload.get("schema_version")
454	    if version != GENERATION_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION:
455	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
456	            f"--resume: generation snapshot schema_version {version!r} != expected "
457	            f"{GENERATION_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION} at {path}; refusing to resume on a stale-shaped "
458	            f"draft set (re-run fresh)"
459	        )
460	    # Codex P2-3: RECURSIVE verdict-key guard over the WHOLE payload (the ITEM-5a section/atom
461	    # metadata is nested) — a leaked verdict at any depth fails loud rather than loading.
462	    try:
463	        _assert_no_verdict_keys_recursive(payload, path=str(path))
464	    except GenerationSnapshotError as exc:
465	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
466	            f"--resume: {exc}. Refusing to load (a resume re-runs every gate; it can NEVER "
467	            "replay a stored decision, §-1.3 ABSOLUTE)."
468	        ) from exc
469	    drafts = payload.get("section_raw_drafts")
470	    if not isinstance(drafts, dict) or not drafts:
471	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
472	            f"--resume: generation snapshot at {path} has no section_raw_drafts; refusing to resume "
473	            f"a generation with no reloadable drafts"
474	        )
475	    # ITEM 5a: the section/atom re-entry metadata MUST be present (a v2 payload always carries
476	    # both keys, possibly empty for an OFF-mode run with no atom-bearing sections). A MISSING key
477	    # (not merely empty) means a malformed/hand-edited payload — fail loud rather than re-enter
478	    # verification with an incomplete structure.
479	    for _meta_key in ("section_plans", "section_atom_catalogs"):
480	        if not isinstance(payload.get(_meta_key), dict):
481	            raise GenerationSnapshotError(
482	                f"--resume: generation snapshot at {path} is missing the ITEM-5a {_meta_key!r} "
483	                "section-metadata map; a resume could not re-enter the verification stage "
484	                "losslessly. Refusing to resume (re-run fresh)."
485	            )
486	    if payload.get("had_contract_sections"):
487	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
488	            f"--resume: generation snapshot at {path} was written for a run that used V30 contract "
489	            "sections; a reloaded plain-SectionPlan outline cannot reconstruct a "
490	            "ContractSectionPlanExt, so a generation-stage resume of this run would diverge from a "
491	            "fresh run. Refusing to resume at the generation stage (re-run fresh, or resume from the "
492	            "corpus_snapshot)."
493	        )
494	    # The cached-draft path produces an EMPTY per-section atom_catalog (only the live _call_section
495	    # builds one). Under PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE != off the post-hoc validator would mark every reloaded
496	    # section degraded ("skipped_empty_catalog") while a fresh run certifies them — a resume != fresh
497	    # DIVERGENCE. Refuse the generation-stage resume outright while that mode is active.
498	    assert_atom_refusal_mode_resumable()
499	    return payload
500	
501	
502	def assert_atom_refusal_mode_resumable() -> None:
503	    """FAIL LOUD if PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE is active (not "off") at resume time.
504	
505	    A generation-stage resume injects cached drafts with NO live atom_catalog. The post-hoc atom-
506	    refusal validator (log_only / strict) consumes that catalog; with it empty, every reloaded
507	    section is flagged "skipped_empty_catalog" / degraded — diverging from a fresh run that has a
508	    real catalog. This refuses the resume rather than silently shipping a spuriously-degraded run
509	    (the divergence the generation snapshot exists to prevent). Mode "off" (the default) never
510	    consults the catalog, so the empty catalog is a true no-op and the resume proceeds.
511	    """
512	    mode = os.environ.get(ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE_ENV, ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE_OFF).strip().lower()
513	    if mode != ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE_OFF:
514	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
515	            f"--resume: {ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE_ENV}={mode!r} is active, but a generation-stage resume "
516	            "injects cached drafts with NO live atom_catalog — the post-hoc atom-refusal validator "
517	            "would mark every reloaded section degraded ('skipped_empty_catalog'), diverging from a "
518	            f"fresh run. Refusing to resume at the generation stage (re-run fresh, or set "
519	            f"{ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE_ENV}=off)."
520	        )
521	
522	
523	def assert_generation_flags_match(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
524	    """FAIL LOUD if the active generation-affecting flags differ from the snapshot slate.
525	
526	    A cached raw draft is a faithful strict_verify input ONLY under the same flag config that
527	    produced it (§-1.3 no-silent-divergence). The verification-only flags are DELIBERATELY NOT in
528	    ``GENERATION_AFFECTING_ENV_FLAGS`` — changing them on a resume is the entire point of the
529	    feature and must be allowed. A mismatch on a generation-affecting flag, however, means the
530	    reloaded draft no longer matches what a fresh run would have produced, so we refuse rather
531	    than ship a divergent run.
532	    """
533	    snapshot_flags = payload.get("generation_flags")
534	    if not isinstance(snapshot_flags, dict):
535	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
536	            "--resume: generation snapshot is missing its generation_flags slate; refusing to "
537	            "resume without the flag config that produced the cached drafts"
538	        )
539	    active = _capture_generation_flags()
540	    mismatches = {
541	        name: {"snapshot": snapshot_flags.get(name, ""), "active": active.get(name, "")}
542	        for name in GENERATION_AFFECTING_ENV_FLAGS
543	        if str(snapshot_flags.get(name, "")) != str(active.get(name, ""))
544	    }
545	    if mismatches:
546	        raise GenerationSnapshotError(
547	            "--resume: generation-affecting flags differ from the snapshot slate "
548	            f"{mismatches}; a cached raw draft is only a faithful strict_verify input under the "
549	            "flag config that produced it. Refusing to resume at the generation stage (re-run fresh)."
550	        )
551	
552	
553	def reconstruct_outline(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Any]:
554	    """Rebuild the list of ``SectionPlan`` dataclasses from a reloaded snapshot payload.
555	
556	    The rehydrated plans are plain ``SectionPlan`` (title / focus / ev_ids / archetype) — never
557	    a ContractSectionPlanExt (the loader already refuses a contract run), so every reloaded
558	    section routes through the LEGACY ``_run_section`` path on resume, exactly matching how a
559	    fresh non-contract run runs. Carries NO verdict — gates re-run on the drafts.
560	    """
561	    # Lazy import keeps this module import-light and avoids a cycle through the generator package.
562	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import SectionPlan
563	
564	    rows = payload.get("outline") or []
565	    plans: list[Any] = []
566	    for row in rows:
567	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
568	            raise GenerationSnapshotError(
569	                f"--resume: generation snapshot outline entry {row!r} is not a JSON object"
570	            )
571	        plans.append(
572	            SectionPlan(
573	                **{k: v for k, v in row.items() if k in SectionPlan.__dataclass_fields__}
574	            )
575	        )
576	    return plans
