1	"""GH I-deepfix-003 (#1374) — junk-deletion grounding gate.
2	
3	Operator rule change 2026-07-09 (CLAUDE.md §-1.3.1): the §-1.3 never-DROP rule is
4	LIFTED for genuine JUNK. Weighting junk to zero is insufficient — a zero-weight row
5	still sits in the evidence pool, the bibliography, and the per-claim corroboration
6	counts, where it pollutes a clinical report (a bot-detection page anchoring a claim; a
7	predatory PDF padding the count; an off-topic law/tourism source). Two classes are
8	hard-DELETED from the run-level grounding pool BEFORE generation:
9	
10	  (a) CHROME NON-SOURCES — bot/captcha/cookie/404/login/empty pages. A failed fetch is
11	      not a source. The content-integrity detector stamps ``row["content_integrity_junk"]``
12	      upstream (the re-tier seam); this gate deletes the stamped rows.
13	  (b) SEMANTICALLY-CONFIRMED OFF-TOPIC whole sources — a source a SEMANTIC topic judge
14	      confirmed off-topic to the research question. DEFAULT path (Fix 1,
15	      ``is_row_deletable_offtopic``) deletes ONLY on the topic judge's affirmative
16	      OFF_SUBJECT stamp: a weight/reranker ``content_relevance_label``
17	      (``demoted`` / ``escalated_demoted``) can NEVER delete, and a positive relevance
18	      verdict (``relevant`` / ``escalated_relevant``) vetoes deletion UNCONDITIONALLY. Judge
19	      verdict ONLY, FAIL-OPEN (any uncertainty / missing verdict / error => KEEP), never a
20	      lexical/tier/number rule. The legacy weight-label reuse
21	      (``weighted_enrichment._is_confirmed_offtopic`` via ``is_row_confirmed_offtopic``) is
22	      reachable only behind ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY=0`` (byte-identical OFF).
23	
24	STILL BINDING (§-1.3): credible ON-TOPIC sources, even low-tier / social / non-journal,
25	are NEVER deleted here (they are neither content-junk nor confirmed-off-topic). An
26	off-topic SPAN inside an on-topic source is handled at compose (the span screen), not
27	here. Duplicates consolidate elsewhere. The faithfulness engine is UNTOUCHED — deleting a
28	row can only SHRINK the grounding pool, so a claim citing a deleted junk row FAILS
29	strict_verify (it can never make one PASS). A deleted junk row was never a valid anchor.
30	
31	MARQUEE/CONTRACT EXEMPTION: a required-entity / contract-bound anchor is on-topic by
32	construction and is NEVER deleted (its evidence_id is in ``exempt_ids``), mirroring the
33	topic gate's existing anchor exemption.
34	
35	Deletion is DISCLOSED: every deleted row is RETURNED with its reason for the manifest + a
36	LOUD log + a Methods line (fail loud, never silent — LAW II).
37	
38	Pure leaf module: no network, no model import, no faithfulness-engine import. Two env
39	kill-switches (LAW VI), DEFAULT ON. When the corpus has zero junk / off-topic rows the
40	groundable pool is byte-identical to the input.
41	"""
42	from __future__ import annotations
43	
44	import logging
45	import os
46	from typing import Any, Iterable, Mapping
47	
48	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.junk_deletion_gate")
49	
50	_CHROME_FLAG = "PG_DELETE_CHROME_NONSOURCE"
51	_OFFTOPIC_FLAG = "PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_SOURCE"
52	# Fix 1 (I-deepfix-003 over-deletion): the off-topic DELETE fires ONLY on the topic judge's
53	# affirmative OFF_SUBJECT stamp; a weight/reranker ``content_relevance_label`` can NEVER
54	# delete. DEFAULT ON. OFF => byte-identical legacy weight-label path.
55	_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY_FLAG = "PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY"
56	# Fix 2 (I-deepfix-003 over-deletion): the off-topic DELETE fires ONLY on an OFF_SUBJECT
57	# stamp THIS run's topic judge produced. A STALE stamp reloaded from an earlier run's
58	# corpus_snapshot (the judge did NOT re-run this pass) demote-KEEPs. DEFAULT ON. OFF =>
59	# freshness is NOT enforced (byte-identical to the Fix-1-only path: any OFF_SUBJECT stamp
60	# deletes regardless of provenance).
61	_FRESH_VERDICT_FLAG = "PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_FRESH_VERDICT_ONLY"
62	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", ""})
63	
64	# An affirmative positive-relevance verdict from the content-relevance judge. A row carrying
65	# one is on-topic by the judge's own word and VETOES deletion unconditionally.
66	_POSITIVE_RELEVANCE_LABELS = frozenset({"relevant", "escalated_relevant"})
67	# String forms that mean an affirmative OFF_SUBJECT stamp (the deletable class of the OFF
68	# split). ``topic_off_subject`` is normally the bool True (Fix 3 sidecar); these tolerate a
69	# string representation without ever matching OFF_ASPECT or a weight label.
70	_OFF_SUBJECT_TRUE_TOKENS = frozenset({"off_subject", "offsubject", "true", "yes", "on", "1"})
71	
72	
73	def chrome_deletion_enabled() -> bool:
74	    """Kill-switch for the chrome-non-source deletion. DEFAULT ON. Falsey =>
75	    stamped-junk rows are KEPT (byte-identical to pre-carve-out)."""
76	    return os.getenv(_CHROME_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
77	
78	
79	def offtopic_deletion_enabled() -> bool:
80	    """Kill-switch for the confirmed-off-topic-source deletion. DEFAULT ON. Falsey =>
81	    confirmed-off-topic sources are KEPT (they fall back to the weight/ledger demote)."""
82	    return os.getenv(_OFFTOPIC_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
83	
84	
85	def topic_judge_only_deletion_enabled() -> bool:
86	    """Kill-switch ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY`` (DEFAULT ON). ON => an off-topic
87	    DELETE fires ONLY on the topic judge's affirmative OFF_SUBJECT stamp
88	    (``is_row_deletable_offtopic``): a weight/reranker ``content_relevance_label`` can NEVER
89	    delete, and a positive relevance verdict vetoes unconditionally. OFF => byte-identical
90	    legacy path (``is_row_confirmed_offtopic``, the weight-label reuse that hard-deleted 197
91	    on-topic rows in the I-deepfix-003 pass)."""
92	    return os.getenv(_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
93	
94	
95	def fresh_verdict_only_deletion_enabled() -> bool:
96	    """Kill-switch ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_FRESH_VERDICT_ONLY`` (DEFAULT ON, Fix 2). ON => an
97	    off-topic DELETE fires ONLY on an OFF_SUBJECT stamp THIS run's topic judge produced (the
98	    caller passes the fresh evidence_id set); a STALE snapshot stamp (reloaded from an earlier
99	    run, judge did NOT re-run) demote-KEEPs instead of deleting. OFF => freshness is NOT
100	    checked (byte-identical to the Fix-1-only path — any OFF_SUBJECT stamp is deletable). No
101	    effect when the caller passes ``fresh_off_subject_ids=None`` (freshness un-enforced)."""
102	    return os.getenv(_FRESH_VERDICT_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
103	
104	
105	def is_row_content_junk(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
106	    """True iff the content-integrity detector stamped this row as a chrome non-source
107	    (bot / cookie / 404 / login / empty). FAIL-OPEN: any error / missing / falsey flag
108	    => NOT junk (row KEPT). A predicate bug must NEVER delete a source."""
109	    try:
110	        v = row.get("content_integrity_junk")
111	        return bool(v) and str(v).strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
112	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never delete on a predicate bug
113	        logger.warning(
114	            "[junk_deletion_gate] content-junk predicate errored (%s) — row KEPT "
115	            "(fail-open).", str(exc)[:160],
116	        )
117	        return False
118	
119	
120	def is_row_confirmed_offtopic(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
121	    """True iff a SEMANTIC topic judge confirmed this WHOLE source off-topic to the
122	    research question. Reuses ``weighted_enrichment._is_confirmed_offtopic`` — the single
123	    source of truth the compose off-topic screen also uses — so the gate and the screen
124	    can never disagree. FAIL-OPEN: an unjudged / ``relevant`` / ``escalated_relevant`` /
125	    missing-verdict / error row is NOT off-topic (row KEPT)."""
126	    try:
127	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: PLC0415
128	            _is_confirmed_offtopic,
129	        )
130	        return bool(_is_confirmed_offtopic(row))
131	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never delete on a predicate bug
132	        logger.warning(
133	            "[junk_deletion_gate] offtopic predicate errored (%s) — row KEPT "
134	            "(fail-open).", str(exc)[:160],
135	        )
136	        return False
137	
138	
139	def _stamped_off_subject(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
140	    """True iff the topic judge stamped this WHOLE source OFF_SUBJECT — a clearly different
141	    subject (the DELETABLE class of the OFF split, e.g. a scholar-mill / tourism / religion
142	    page in an AI-labor report). The Fix-3 sidecar is the bool ``topic_off_subject=True``; a
143	    string verdict ``topic_relevance_verdict == "OFF_SUBJECT"`` is tolerated as the same
144	    signal. Legacy ``topic_offtopic_demoted`` alone (which conflates OFF_ASPECT 'same entity,
145	    wrong aspect' with OFF_SUBJECT) does NOT count — a demote-keep aspect verdict is never a
146	    delete trigger, so only the explicit subject-level stamp deletes."""
147	    v = row.get("topic_off_subject")
148	    if v is True:
149	        return True
150	    if isinstance(v, str) and v.strip().lower() in _OFF_SUBJECT_TRUE_TOKENS:
151	        return True
152	    verdict = str(row.get("topic_relevance_verdict", "") or "").strip().lower()
153	    return verdict == "off_subject"
154	
155	
156	def is_row_deletable_offtopic(
157	    row: Mapping[str, Any],
158	    fresh_off_subject_ids: "Iterable[str] | None" = None,
159	) -> bool:
160	    """DELETE predicate for the confirmed-off-topic class (topic-judge-only path, Fix 1 + Fix 2).
161	
162	    Returns True (row is DELETABLE) iff the topic judge AFFIRMATIVELY stamped this WHOLE
163	    source OFF_SUBJECT. Three hard guarantees keep this from over-deleting:
164	
165	    * A weight/reranker ``content_relevance_label`` (``demoted`` / ``escalated_demoted``) is
166	      NEVER a delete trigger — those are weight-demote-to-0.25 KEEP labels (a Qwen3-Reranker
167	      numeric score / a GLM 'INSUFFICIENT' entailment), NOT topic verdicts. Reusing them as a
168	      DELETE trigger is exactly what hard-deleted 197 on-topic rows (St. Louis Fed, OECD, ILO,
169	      McKinsey, HBS, Wikipedia, 23 T1 journal papers) in the I-deepfix-003 pass.
170	    * An affirmative positive-relevance verdict (``content_relevance_label`` in
171	      {relevant, escalated_relevant}) VETOES deletion UNCONDITIONALLY — even against a
172	      stale/false OFF stamp. When the two judges disagree, the positive relevance wins.
173	    * Fix 2 (fresh-verdict-only): when ``fresh_off_subject_ids`` is supplied (a concrete set —
174	      the evidence_ids THIS run's topic judge freshly confirmed OFF_SUBJECT) AND
175	      ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_FRESH_VERDICT_ONLY`` is ON, a row whose OFF_SUBJECT stamp is NOT in
176	      that set is a STALE snapshot stamp (an earlier run's verdict reloaded from
177	      corpus_snapshot) and demote-KEEPs — only a fresh verdict deletes. ``None`` (the default)
178	      => freshness is un-enforced (byte-identical to the Fix-1-only path).
179	
180	    FAIL-OPEN: not a Mapping / no OFF_SUBJECT stamp / stale-only stamp / missing verdict / any
181	    error => NOT deletable (row KEPT). A predicate bug must NEVER delete a source."""
182	    try:
183	        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
184	            return False
185	        label = str(row.get("content_relevance_label", "") or "").strip().lower()
186	        if label in _POSITIVE_RELEVANCE_LABELS:
187	            return False  # affirmative positive-relevance verdict — KEEP (unconditional veto)
188	        if not _stamped_off_subject(row):
189	            return False
190	        # Fix 2: a STALE snapshot OFF_SUBJECT stamp (not in this run's fresh set) demote-KEEPs.
191	        # Enforced only when the caller passes a concrete set AND the flag is ON.
192	        if fresh_off_subject_ids is not None and fresh_verdict_only_deletion_enabled():
193	            eid = str(row.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
194	            if eid not in {str(e) for e in fresh_off_subject_ids}:
195	                return False  # stale stamp — demote-keep, never delete on an old verdict
196	        return True
197	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never delete on a predicate bug
198	        logger.warning(
199	            "[junk_deletion_gate] deletable-offtopic predicate errored (%s) — row KEPT "
200	            "(fail-open).", str(exc)[:160],
201	        )
202	        return False
203	
204	
205	def partition_rows(
206	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
207	    exempt_ids: "Iterable[str] | None" = None,
208	    fresh_off_subject_ids: "Iterable[str] | None" = None,
209	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
210	    """Split the run-level grounding pool (``evidence_for_gen``) into
211	    ``(kept, deleted)``.
212	
213	    A row is DELETED iff it is a chrome non-source (kill-switch A) OR a
214	    confirmed-off-topic source (kill-switch B), AND its ``evidence_id`` is NOT in
215	    ``exempt_ids`` (a marquee / contract-bound anchor is on-topic by construction and is
216	    never deleted). Each deleted row carries a ``deletion_reason`` for disclosure.
217	
218	    Order-preserving; never mutates the input rows (a deleted row is copied before the
219	    reason is stamped); idempotent (re-running on a clean pool returns it unchanged with
220	    an empty deleted list). When BOTH kill-switches are OFF, returns ``(list(rows), [])``
221	    — byte-identical.
222	    """
223	    chrome_on = chrome_deletion_enabled()
224	    offtopic_on = offtopic_deletion_enabled()
225	    if not chrome_on and not offtopic_on:
226	        return list(rows), []
227	    exempt = {str(e) for e in (exempt_ids or ())}
228	    # Fix 2: materialize the fresh OFF_SUBJECT id set ONCE (or None to leave freshness
229	    # un-enforced). A stale-stamp row whose id is absent demote-KEEPs (see
230	    # is_row_deletable_offtopic).
231	    fresh_ids = None if fresh_off_subject_ids is None else {str(e) for e in fresh_off_subject_ids}
232	    kept: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
233	    deleted: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
234	    for row in rows:
235	        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
236	            kept.append(row)
237	            continue
238	        eid = str(row.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
239	        if eid and eid in exempt:
240	            kept.append(row)  # marquee / contract anchor — NEVER deleted
241	            continue
242	        reason = ""
243	        if chrome_on and is_row_content_junk(row):
244	            reason = "content_integrity_junk:" + str(
245	                row.get("content_integrity_class", "chrome") or "chrome"
246	            )
247	        elif offtopic_on:
248	            # Fix 1: default-ON path deletes ONLY on the topic judge's OFF_SUBJECT stamp
249	            # (weight labels can never delete; positive relevance vetoes). The legacy
250	            # weight-label predicate stays reachable ONLY behind the OFF kill-switch so
251	            # ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY=0`` is byte-identical to pre-Fix-1.
252	            if topic_judge_only_deletion_enabled():
253	                if is_row_deletable_offtopic(row, fresh_off_subject_ids=fresh_ids):
254	                    reason = "confirmed_offtopic_subject"
255	            elif is_row_confirmed_offtopic(row):
256	                reason = "confirmed_offtopic"
257	        if reason:
258	            copied = dict(row)
259	            copied["deletion_reason"] = reason
260	            deleted.append(copied)
261	        else:
262	            kept.append(row)
263	    return kept, deleted
264	
265	
266	def _deletion_signal(reason: str) -> str:
267	    """Human-readable 'which signal fired' derived from the deletion_reason — a chrome class
268	    or the topic-judge OFF_SUBJECT stamp. Keeps the disclosure honest about the exact
269	    category (chrome non-source vs confirmed-off-topic) that removed each row."""
270	    if reason.startswith("content_integrity_junk"):
271	        cls = reason.split(":", 1)[1] if ":" in reason else "chrome"
272	        return "chrome:" + (cls or "chrome")
273	    if reason.startswith("confirmed_offtopic"):
274	        return "topic_judge_off_subject"
275	    return reason or "unknown"
276	
277	
278	def _judge_verdict_summary(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
279	    """Compact per-source verdict trail the row carried at deletion time (read-only — never a
280	    delete trigger here). Surfaces WHY the source was judged, so a reviewer can second-guess
281	    any deletion from the manifest alone."""
282	    parts: list[str] = []
283	    if _stamped_off_subject(row):
284	        parts.append("topic=OFF_SUBJECT")
285	    if row.get("topic_offtopic_demoted") is True:
286	        parts.append("topic_offtopic_demoted=True")
287	    label = str(row.get("content_relevance_label", "") or "").strip()
288	    if label:
289	        parts.append("content_relevance_label=" + label)
290	    verdict = str(row.get("topic_relevance_verdict", "") or "").strip()
291	    if verdict:
292	        parts.append("topic_relevance_verdict=" + verdict)
293	    ci_class = str(row.get("content_integrity_class", "") or "").strip()
294	    if ci_class:
295	        parts.append("content_integrity_class=" + ci_class)
296	    return ";".join(parts)
297	
298	
299	def disclosure_records(deleted_rows: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
300	    """Compact disclosure rows for the run telemetry — the audit trail that proves the
301	    pipeline found and DELETED each junk / off-topic source (recorded, never silent).
302	
303	    Fix 5 (records half): each record now carries the per-source SIGNAL that fired
304	    (chrome class vs topic-judge OFF_SUBJECT), the JUDGE VERDICT trail, and the source TIER
305	    alongside the existing evidence_id / title / url / deletion_reason — enough to second-
306	    guess any single deletion straight from the manifest (fail-loud, never silent)."""
307	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
308	    for row in deleted_rows:
309	        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
310	            continue
311	        reason = str(row.get("deletion_reason", "") or "")
312	        out.append({
313	            "evidence_id": row.get("evidence_id", ""),
314	            "title": row.get("source_title") or row.get("title") or "",
315	            "url": row.get("url") or row.get("source_url") or "",
316	            "tier": row.get("tier") or row.get("source_tier") or "",
317	            "deletion_reason": reason,
318	            "signal": _deletion_signal(reason),
319	            "judge_verdict": _judge_verdict_summary(row),
320	            "excluded_from_grounding": True,
321	        })
322	    return out
323

content.content:
1	"""Verified-only extractive executive summary (I-meta-002-q1d #949 part b).
2	
3	Frontier DR reports lead with a key-findings-up-front summary; POLARIS opened cold into Efficacy. This
4	builds a "Key Findings" block by EXTRACTING the first verified sentence (verbatim, with its `[N]` citation)
5	from each verified section. It is PURELY EXTRACTIVE — it copies sentences that already survived strict_verify
6	and introduces ZERO new claims, no LLM call, no spend. Empty input → "" (no empty heading).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import contextvars
12	import logging
13	import os
14	import re
15	from typing import Any, Callable
16	
17	# Module logger for the FF3 render-truncation ``[activation]`` markers (I-deepfix-001 Wave-5 #1344).
18	# A child logger => it propagates to root, so the Gate-B ``_ActivationMarkerCaptureHandler`` (attached to
19	# the ROOT logger for the in-process query) captures the marker lines exactly like the other 10 modules.
20	_MODULE_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
21	
22	# One sentence = minimal run up to end punctuation, PLUS any trailing `[N]` citation marker(s), where the
23	# end punctuation must be a real sentence boundary: followed by whitespace+capital/bracket/digit OR end of
24	# text. The boundary lookahead prevents stopping inside a decimal ("2.1" — the period is followed by a digit,
25	# no whitespace, so it is not a boundary). Matching (not splitting) keeps trailing-citation forms (`claim.
26	# [1]` AND `claim [1].`) attached to the sentence — re.split would consume the trailing `[N]` (Codex
27	# diff-gate iter-1 P2).
28	_SENTENCE_RE = re.compile(r".+?[.!?](?:\s*\[\d+\])*(?=\s+[A-Z(\[\d]|\s*$)", re.DOTALL)
29	
30	# A Key Finding is a SPAN-VERIFIED statement — by definition it carries its `[N]` / `[#ev:`
31	# citation (module docstring). This is the robust per-SENTENCE gap filter (I-gen-006 #1178
32	# C07/P07): a gap-disclosure sentence ("... did not survive strict verification; curator-
33	# actionable gap.") carries NO citation, so in a MIXED V30 section (a leading gap slot +
34	# later verified prose, where the SECTION still has sentences_verified>0) the uncited gap
35	# sentence is skipped and the first CITED sentence is lifted instead. Keys on the citation
36	# invariant, never on matching gap-disclosure text.
37	_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]|\[#ev:")
38	
39	# Gap-disclosure boilerplate (I-gen-006 #1178 C07/P07, Codex iter-5): the V30 contract-runner
40	# gap disclosure is a FIXED two-sentence template — "Contract-bound content ... curator-actionable
41	# gap. See manifest.frame_coverage_report and human_gap_tasks.json for per-entity detail.[N]" — and
42	# its SECOND sentence DOES carry a `[N]` (a pointer to the gap-task sidecar, NOT an evidence span),
43	# so the citation filter alone cannot exclude it. A Key Finding must be a span-verified CLAIM, never
44	# a gap pointer; exclude any sentence carrying a canonical gap-disclosure marker. Robust because the
45	# disclosure text is generated from fixed constants (contract_section_runner / _GAP_STUB_SENTENCE),
46	# never free-form prose — this is a rendering filter, not a §-1.1 quality-by-pattern judgement.
47	_GAP_MARKER_RE = re.compile(
48	    r"curator-actionable gap|did not survive strict verification|"
49	    r"did not survive (?:4-role )?verification|frame_coverage_report|human_gap_tasks|"
50	    # N4 (I-deepfix-001 wave-2): the plain-English gap disclosure carries a [N] so
51	    # the citation filter alone cannot exclude it — recognize it here too.
52	    r"insufficient verified evidence",
53	    re.IGNORECASE,
54	)
55	
56	# An ATX markdown header: 1-6 '#' followed by whitespace ("### Section"). Used to detect a
57	# leaked section header WITHOUT mis-classifying hash-leading prose like "#1 ranked" (Codex P2).
58	_ATX_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"#{1,6}\s")
59	
60	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", ""})
61	
62	# How many leading verified sentences to lift from each section (default 1 — the headline finding).
63	_SENTENCES_PER_SECTION = 1
64	# Hard cap on total bullets so the summary stays a summary.
65	_MAX_BULLETS = 6
66	
67	# I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 2/3 — shared render hygiene used by Key Findings (here) AND the
68	# Abstract/Conclusion harvesters (abstract_conclusion.py imports these). PURE string ops; they
69	# only change which already-verified sentence RENDERS or trim a marker RUN — never a verdict, never
70	# a source/count. Faithfulness-STRENGTHENING (they can only suppress a fragment, never promote one).
71	
72	# Trailing `[N]` / `[#ev:...]` citation markers (stripped before the truncation test so a clean
73	# "…claim.[12]" is judged on the "." not the marker).
74	_TRAILING_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"(?:\s*\[(?:\d+|#ev:[^\]]*)\])+\s*$")
75	# HIGH-PRECISION mid-word / cut-span truncation MARKERS only (the §-1.1 over-strip ban — never a
76	# heuristic guess at a cut word): a dangling/closed ellipsis (`…`, `...`, `[...]`, `[…]`, a dangling
77	# `[...` whose `]` was capped) or a trailing mid-word hyphen. An INTERNAL hyphen
78	# ("treatment-specific effects were observed.") is NOT a truncation and still renders.
79	_TRUNCATION_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[\s*(?:\.\.\.|…)\s*\]?\s*$|(?:…|\.\.\.)\s*$|-\s*$")
80	# A run of 2+ ADJACENT numeric citation markers ("[12][13][14]" / "[12] [13]") — capped to the
81	# first N (document order = the body's own priority). Non-adjacent markers belong to DISTINCT
82	# in-sentence claims and are never merged/capped.
83	_ADJACENT_MARKER_RUN_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\](?:\s*\[\d+\])+")
84	
85	# Default per-run citation cap (LAW VI override PG_KEY_FINDINGS_MAX_MARKERS / the conclusion uses
86	# its own override). A summary line citing >3 sources in one run is render-noise; the body + the
87	# bibliography retain every reference, so capping the SUMMARY display can never orphan a citation.
88	_DEFAULT_MAX_MARKERS = 3
89	
90	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
91	# I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3a — CORPUS-GROUNDED boundary span-cut (the UNBLINDING).
92	#
93	# The legacy ``is_truncated_fragment`` only matched an explicit trailing ellipsis / hyphen MARKER,
94	# so it returned False on the dominant truncation shape in a real render: a span CUT mid-word right
95	# before its ``[N]`` citation ("… 1.2 Resea.[14]", "… incorporates the ap.[5]"). This adds the
96	# proven detector rule (scripts/iwire013_sec11_forensic_audit.py): a boundary token is a span cut
97	# iff it is NOT a word the run's OWN corpus uses AND it is a strict NON-inflectional prefix (end cut)
98	# / suffix (start cut) of a LONGER corpus word. The corpus-vocabulary allowlist (``known_words``,
99	# built by the caller from evidence_pool direct_quote/statement/title) is the FALSE-POSITIVE GUARD:
100	# a real-but-rare sentence-ender ("classifier", "computerisation") is either known or has no longer
101	# known completion, so it does NOT flag, while a real cut ("Resea"→"research") always does. The
102	# completion gate keeps precision high (the detector holds ~2% FP on the banked render).
103	#
104	# DROP-PATH SAFE / BACKWARD-COMPATIBLE: ``known_words`` is keyword-only and defaults to ``None`` —
105	# every existing caller (no corpus) gets BYTE-IDENTICAL legacy behaviour (the marker check only).
106	# The boundary check fires ONLY when a corpus allowlist is supplied AND the caller marks which
107	# boundary (``ends_before_marker`` / ``starts_after_marker``) is eligible — so a complete sentence's
108	# trailing complete word is never end-checked unless the caller says a marker follows it.
109	
110	# An alphabetic word token (the boundary word; mirrors the detector's _WORD_RE).
111	_BOUNDARY_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'\-]*[A-Za-z]|[A-Za-z]")
112	# Leading `[N]` / `[#ev:...]` citation markers (stripped before reading the first word after a cut).
113	_LEADING_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"^(?:\s*\[(?:\d+|#ev:[^\]]*)\])+")
114	# Suffixes that make a longer known word a mere INFLECTION of the token (so the token is the real
115	# base word, not a cut): 'disadvantage' -> {'disadvantaged','disadvantages'} only => NOT a cut. A
116	# real END cut has a NON-inflectional completion ('resea' -> 'research' = 'resea'+'rch').
117	_INFLECTION_SUFFIXES = ("s", "d", "es", "ed", "ing", "ly", "ic")
118	# Single-letter boundary tokens that are legitimate one-letter English words (never a cut).
119	_SINGLE_LETTER_KEEP_TOKENS = frozenset({"a", "i"})

165	def _boundary_last_word(text: str) -> str:
166	    """The trailing alphabetic word of ``text`` (a single artificial '.' a span-truncator appends,
167	    plus a trailing hyphen/quote, are stripped first). '' when there is no trailing word."""
168	    s = text.rstrip().rstrip('"”\')')
169	    if s.endswith("."):
170	        s = s[:-1].rstrip()
171	    m = re.search(r"([A-Za-z][A-Za-z'\-]*)$", s)
172	    return m.group(1).strip("-'") if m else ""

176	    """The leading alphabetic word of ``text`` (after any leading citation marker is stripped by the
177	    caller). '' when there is no leading word."""
178	    m = re.match(r"\s*([A-Za-z][A-Za-z'\-]*)", text)
179	    return m.group(1).strip("-'") if m else ""

220	# FF3-TRUNC-SEM complement-DEMANDING connectives — tokens that grammar guarantees CANNOT terminate a
221	# complete clause, so a sentence ending on one (with NO terminal punctuation) is a SEMANTICALLY-truncated
222	# fragment even though it reads as plausible prose. These are DELIBERATELY distinct from FF2's lexical sets
223	# (they are legitimate short tokens ELSEWHERE — the exact reason FF2's last-word lists miss them):
224	#   * dangling COMPARATIVE — demands a second operand ("… adoption spread faster than", "… arm A versus").
225	#   * cut SUBORDINATOR — demands its clause ("… the effect held unless", "… unclear whether").
226	#   * open APPOSITIVE / list lead-in — demands the enumerated item ("… several factors namely",
227	#     "… drivers such").
228	# HIGH-PRECISION on purpose (§-1.3 over-strip is worse than a leak): only tokens with NO valid
229	# sentence-final use are listed; ambiguous prepositions ("to"/"toward"/"with") that A2 keeps as valid
230	# enders are EXCLUDED. NOUNS never enter this set.
231	_FF3_TRAILING_COMPLEMENT_DEMAND = frozenset({
232	    "than", "versus", "vs",        # dangling comparative
233	    "whether", "unless",           # cut subordinator
234	    "namely", "such",              # open appositive / list lead-in
235	})

238	def _is_semantically_truncated(core: str) -> bool:
239	    """FF3-TRUNC-SEM detector: True iff ``core`` ends on a complement-DEMANDING connective that grammar
240	    guarantees cannot terminate a complete clause (a dangling comparative / cut subordinator / open
241	    appositive). The caller gates this on ABSENT terminal punctuation (a full-stopped clause is complete
242	    and FAIL-OPEN kept, §-1.3). PURE — never repairs, never fabricates, only classifies.
243	
244	    Precision guards (over-strip is worse than a leak):
245	      * >= 2 boundary tokens (a real clause, not a bare 1-word stub).
246	      * an UPPERCASE trailing acronym / single-CAPITAL label is a valid ender, never a semantic cut.
247	      * a demander preceded by a PRONOUN / demonstrative subject marks a legitimate elided tail
248	        ("… as many as it") — KEPT (mirrors the FF2 copula keep-guard)."""
249	    tokens = _BOUNDARY_WORD_RE.findall(core)
250	    if len(tokens) < 2:
251	        return False
252	    raw_last = tokens[-1]
253	    # A trailing ALL-CAPS acronym / single-CAPITAL label ("… per protocol PP", "… vitamin C") is a valid
254	    # ender, never a dangling connective (str.isupper() is True only when every cased char is uppercase).
255	    if raw_last.isupper():
256	        return False
257	    if raw_last.lower() not in _FF3_TRAILING_COMPLEMENT_DEMAND:
258	        return False
259	    # "as such" is a valid sentence-final idiom ("… was classified AS SUCH", "… recognized AS SUCH"),
260	    # NOT the "such as" list lead-in FF3 targets ("… key drivers SUCH [as X]"). The two are distinguished
261	    # by the token BEFORE "such": "as" => the complete idiom (KEEP); a content noun => the cut lead-in
262	    # (flag). Codex Wave-5 P1; §-1.3 over-strip is worse than a leak.
263	    if raw_last.lower() == "such" and tokens[-2].lower() == "as":
264	        return False
265	    # keep a legitimate elided/comparative tail whose demander sits right after a pronoun subject.
266	    if tokens[-2].lower() in _COPULA_SUBJECT_PRONOUN_KEEP:
267	        return False
268	    return True

271	# ── Wave-5 realized-effect telemetry (the honest [activation] marker counts) ──────────────────────────
272	# HONEST-LIVENESS: each guard is a DETECT-and-DROP render screen — returning True is the drop signal every
273	# render-seam consumer honors (a truncated fragment is removed from the render). So detected == dropped and
274	# repaired is ALWAYS 0 (§-1.3: the guard NEVER fabricates a word to complete a fragment — an unsafe-to-
275	# repair stub is dropped, never "repaired"). ``failopen`` counts a guard-INTERNAL fault (the detection
276	# logic raised): the leg then FAILS OPEN (keeps the fragment, never crashes the render) and the emit
277	# surfaces a DISTINCT ``unavailable_failopen`` marker the run_gate_b canary REJECTS. Counts are DETECTION
278	# EVENTS (a fragment screened at multiple render seams counts once per screen) — a liveness signal, never a
279	# quality/threshold gate (the canary accepts detected=0). Reset per-report by the Gate-B entrypoint.
280	#
281	# TASK-LOCAL (Codex Wave-5 P1): the counters live in a ``contextvars.ContextVar`` per FF-leg, NOT a module-
282	# global dict, so a concurrent in-process query (each ``run_gate_b_query`` runs as its own asyncio Task with
283	# its own copied context) can never interleave another query's reset / detect / emit into false marker
284	# counts or a wiped fail-open. ``reset`` binds a FRESH dict into the CURRENT context via ``.set`` — that
285	# ``.set`` (not an in-place zero of a shared object) is what isolates the counter: each per-report Task gets
286	# its own binding, its child ``run_one_query`` Task inherits that dict by reference (detect mutates it), and
287	# the same Task's ``emit`` reads it back. The ContextVar default is ``None`` (never a shared mutable default,
288	# which would re-introduce the global-dict bug); a context that never called reset lazily gets its own zeroed
289	# dict on first touch.
290	def _new_trunc_counters() -> "dict[str, int]":
291	    """A fresh zeroed FF3 counter dict. ``screened`` (I-deepfix-001 Wave-5 reviewer P0) is the LIVENESS
292	    counter — how many fragments the guard actually EXAMINED at the render seam, incremented whether or not
293	    a leg fired. The emitted marker's ``reached`` boolean is ``screened > 0``, so a flag-ON-but-never-reached
294	    (still-dark) guard can no longer pass the activation canary on a bare ``detected=0`` marker. ``screened``
295	    is a pure liveness/observability signal — it is NOT a quality/detection count and gates NOTHING about the
296	    report contents (§-1.3): ``detected=0`` is still an accepted eligible-yet-zero fire when ``reached`` is
297	    True."""
298	    return {"screened": 0, "detected": 0, "repaired": 0, "dropped": 0, "failopen": 0}
299

302	    "ff3_trunc_telemetry", default=None
303	)

308	    d = _FF3_TRUNC_TELEMETRY_VAR.get()
309	    if d is None:
310	        d = _new_trunc_counters()
311	        _FF3_TRUNC_TELEMETRY_VAR.set(d)

342	def emit_truncation_activation_markers(logger: "logging.Logger | None" = None) -> None:
343	    """Emit the FF3 realized-effect ``[activation]`` marker ONCE per report (called by the Gate-B
344	    entrypoint AFTER render, within the in-process query so the marker reaches the activation-canary
345	    capture buffer). It fires ONLY when the FF3 flag is ON (OFF => no line => byte-identical).
346	
347	    FALSE-GREEN FIX (I-deepfix-001 Wave-5 reviewer P0): the marker leads with ``reached=<True|False>``, the
348	    LIVENESS proof that the guard's render seam actually invoked ``is_truncated_fragment`` this report
349	    (``reached`` = ``screened > 0``). The canary bool-checks ``reached=True`` — so a flag-ON guard that
350	    stayed DARK (the render seam never called it) emits ``reached=False`` and FAILS the canary, closing the
351	    false-green where a bare ``detected=0`` marker passed a guard that never ran. This is a BOOLEAN liveness
352	    check, NOT a count threshold: a realized ``detected=0`` with ``reached=True`` is still an HONEST
353	    ran-ok-zero the canary ACCEPTS (§-1.3 never gate the report on a detection count). A guard-internal

355	    into the caller (a telemetry emit must never abort a render)."""

362	                bool(_ff3["screened"]),

364	                _ff3["detected"],
365	                _ff3["repaired"],
366	                _ff3["dropped"],
367	            )
368	            if _ff3["failopen"] > 0:

370	    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - a telemetry emit must never abort a render

390	def _boundary_token_is_span_cut(
391	    token: str, known_words: "set[str] | frozenset[str]", *, mode: str
392	) -> bool:
393	    """A boundary token is a span cut iff it is NOT a known corpus word AND it is a strict prefix
394	    (end cut) / suffix (start cut) of a LONGER known corpus word. The completion gate keeps
395	    precision high: a legit-but-rare sentence-ender is either known or has no longer known
396	    completion, so it does NOT flag; a real span cut ('Resea'->'research') always does. A len-1
397	    token also requires a longer-corpus-word completion (so a legit one-letter finding survives);
398	    a len-2 token keeps an abbreviation allowlist."""
399	    if not token or not known_words:
400	        return False
401	    t = token.lower()
402	    completes = (
403	        _known_word_has_longer_prefix(t, known_words) if mode == "end"
404	        else _known_word_has_longer_suffix(t, known_words)
405	    )
406	    if len(t) == 1:
407	        # I-wire-017 (#1339) FIX A: a LOWERCASE single-letter END boundary token ("At t.[2]",
408	        # "restricted to s.[89]") is a mid-word span cut even when the bare letter is itself a known
409	        # corpus token (footnote/stat markers make "t"/"s" "known") — so this len-1 branch runs
410	        # BEFORE the ``t in known_words`` early-out below. Precision guard (this is §-1.1-lethal):
411	        # the cut is gated on the ORIGINAL token being lowercase, so a legitimate single-CAPITAL
412	        # label finding — "vitamin C [5]", "hepatitis B [8]", "grade B [12]" — never flags (its
413	        # boundary token is uppercase). mode=="end" only (a START-of-unit single letter is too weak
414	        # a signal to drop on). Still gated on the completion test and the {"a","i"} keep-list, per
415	        # the I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3b D-P1-2 rationale (a true cut "research"->"r" flags via
416	        # completion; a legit "type 2 diabetes ... a" survives via {"a","i"}).
417	        if (
418	            mode == "end"
419	            and token[:1].islower()
420	            and t not in _SINGLE_LETTER_KEEP_TOKENS
421	            and completes
422	        ):
423	            return True
424	        if t in known_words:
425	            return False
426	        return t not in _SINGLE_LETTER_KEEP_TOKENS and completes
427	    if t in known_words:
428	        return False
429	    if len(t) == 2:
430	        return t not in _SHORT_OK_BOUNDARY_TOKENS and completes
431	    return completes  # len>=3 and a chopped fragment of a known corpus word -> a span cut

434	def is_truncated_fragment(
435	    text: str,
436	    known_words: "set[str] | frozenset[str] | None" = None,
437	    *,
438	    ends_before_marker: bool = False,
439	    starts_after_marker: bool = False,
440	) -> bool:
441	    """True iff ``text`` carries a mid-word / cut-span truncation.
442	
443	    Two independent, drop-path-safe signals:
444	      1. UNAMBIGUOUS MARKER (always, no corpus needed): a trailing/closed ellipsis or a trailing
445	         mid-word hyphen, after stripping trailing ``[N]`` citation markers. Never guesses at a cut
446	         word from letters alone — a complete sentence with an internal hyphen still passes.
447	      2. CORPUS-GROUNDED BOUNDARY SPAN-CUT (I-wire-013 #1327, only when ``known_words`` is supplied):
448	         the boundary token before a ``[N]`` (``ends_before_marker``) or the lowercase token after
449	         one (``starts_after_marker``) is a non-inflectional prefix/suffix of a LONGER corpus word
450	         and is itself absent from the corpus — e.g. "… 1.2 Resea.[14]". The corpus allowlist is the
451	         false-positive guard (a real, complete word is known or has no longer completion).
452	
453	    BACKWARD-COMPATIBLE: ``known_words=None`` (the default for every legacy caller) skips ONLY the
454	    corpus-allowlist span-cut leg (signal 2); the always-on truncation-marker leg (signal 1) AND the
455	    corpus-INDEPENDENT trailing function-word / dangling-"pp." leg (I-wire Wave-A) still run.
456	
457	    I-deepfix-001 Wave-5 (#1344) adds one OPT-IN, default-OFF render-truncation leg (BYTE-IDENTICAL when
458	    OFF): FF3-TRUNC-SEM (``PG_FF3_TRUNC_SEM`` — a semantic complement-demanding cut a pure lexical last-word
459	    rule misses). It DROPS the fragment (return True), never repairs/fabricates; a fault FAILS OPEN + records
460	    a distinct degrade the Gate-B activation canary rejects. Faithfulness-neutral (no verdict touched). PURE
461	    except the flag-gated detection-event telemetry (a benign counter side effect, active ONLY when the FF3
462	    flag is ON). The FF2-TRUNC-v2 lexical legs (copula/aux + lone-letter) were RETIRED as unsound."""
463	    if not text:
464	        return False
465	    core = _TRAILING_CITATION_RE.sub("", text.strip()).rstrip()
466	    if core and _TRUNCATION_MARKER_RE.search(core):
467	        return True
468	    # A2 (I-wire Wave-A) — corpus-INDEPENDENT trailing function-word / dangling-"pp." span cut. High
469	    # precision (a complete sentence never ends on these), so it runs for EVERY caller (no corpus
470	    # needed) alongside the always-on marker leg above.
471	    if core:
472	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-5 (#1344): the FF3 semantic render-truncation leg is OPT-IN (default OFF => the
473	        # leg below is SKIPPED => byte-identical to the pre-Wave-5 HEAD behaviour). The FF2 lexical legs were
474	        # retired as unsound (see the retirement note below). Read once (LAW VI, call-time) so a per-run
475	        # slate/env change lands without an import-time freeze.
476	        _ff3_on = _ff3_trunc_sem_enabled()
477	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-5 (#1344) FALSE-GREEN FIX (reviewer P0): record that the flag-ON guard ACTUALLY
478	        # examined this fragment at the render seam — the LIVENESS proof the activation canary needs. A guard
479	        # that is flag-ON but whose render seam never called this function leaves ``screened`` at the per-
480	        # report reset zero, so the emitted marker carries ``reached=False`` and the canary REJECTS it; a
481	        # still-dark guard can no longer pass on a bare ``detected=0`` marker. Incremented for EVERY examined
482	        # fragment (before the terminal-punct gate), so a clean report of complete sentences still proves the
483	        # guard ran with ``detected=0`` — reach is proven WITHOUT requiring detected>0. Gated on the flag =>
484	        # OFF is byte-identical (no counter is touched when the guard is disabled).
485	        if _ff3_on:
486	            _note_ff3_truncation_screened()
487	        raw_last = _boundary_last_word(core)
488	        # Codex P1 (Wave-A): an UPPERCASE clinical/statistical acronym or a single-CAPITAL label is a
489	        # VALID sentence ender, never a dangling function word — do NOT lowercase-collide it into the
490	        # article/conjunction set. "… an adjusted OR.[5]" ("or"), "… used ITS.[7]" ("its"), "… had
491	        # type A.[3]" / "… vitamin C.[9]" ("a"/"c") must be KEPT (fail-open per §-1.3, over-strip is
492	        # worse than a leak). ``str.isupper()`` is True only when every cased char is uppercase, so a
493	        # genuinely dangling lowercase "the"/"and"/"its" (mixed/lower case) still flags.
494	        if raw_last and not raw_last.isupper():
495	            last_word = raw_last.lower()
496	            # Codex P1 (Wave-A iter-3): defer to the EXISTING short-token keep-lists. "a"/"i"
497	            # (_SINGLE_LETTER_KEEP_TOKENS) and "an"/"or"/"my" (_SHORT_OK_BOUNDARY_TOKENS) are
498	            # legitimate short sentence enders already whitelisted by the iwire017 fix ("glucose
499	            # a.", "vitamin a.", "hepatitis a."), so the function-word cut MUST NOT override them —
500	            # over-strip is worse than a leak (§-1.3). Only a token that is a dangling function word
501	            # AND is not an accepted short ender ("… defined by the.", "… driven by and.") flags.
502	            if (
503	                last_word in _TRAILING_FUNCTION_WORD_CUT
504	                and last_word not in _SINGLE_LETTER_KEEP_TOKENS
505	                and last_word not in _SHORT_OK_BOUNDARY_TOKENS
506	            ):
507	                return True
508	        # FF2-TRUNC-v2 LEXICAL cut legs (a trailing copula/aux cut + a lone-letter-after-connective cut) —
509	        # RETIRED as UNSOUND (I-deepfix-001; never shipped), for the SAME reason the lone-letter leg was
510	        # already removed at Wave-5 iter-4 (Codex P1). A trailing copula/aux is lexically AMBIGUOUS between a
511	        # value cut ("… the share of workers is") and a grammatically COMPLETE fronted-complement / relative /
512	        # possession / noun-homograph clause ("the tasks that workers do", "the skills workers have", "what
513	        # assets households have", "political will", "a must", "recovered in May"). No last-word keep-set can
514	        # separate the two; a corpus-grounded or semantic signal is required, which a pure last-word list
515	        # lacks. §-1.3 over-strip is the cardinal sin, so both lexical legs are removed entirely. Genuine cuts
516	        # remain covered by the FF3-TRUNC-SEM semantic leg (below) + the always-on corpus-grounded span-cut
517	        # leg (``ends_before_marker`` + ``_boundary_token_is_span_cut``, which flags a chopped "China" -> "C"
518	        # ONLY when it non-inflectionally completes a longer corpus word — the grounded signal the lexical
519	        # allowlist lacked).
520	        # FF3-TRUNC-SEM (inv_5) — SEMANTIC truncation: the clause ends on a complement-DEMANDING connective
521	        # grammar guarantees cannot terminate it (a dangling comparative "…faster than", a cut subordinator
522	        # "…held unless", an open appositive "…factors namely"/"…drivers such"). FF2's lexical last-word
523	        # lists MISS these — the tokens are legitimate short words elsewhere — so FF3 is a SEPARATE flag-
524	        # gated leg (PG_FF3_TRUNC_SEM, default OFF => byte-identical). RENDER-ONLY / FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL:
525	        # it DROPS the unsafe-to-render fragment (returns True), NEVER repairs / fabricates a completion
526	        # (§-1.3). Gated on ABSENT terminal punctuation (fail-open on a full stop). Fault => FAIL OPEN.
527	        if _ff3_on and not _ends_with_terminal_punct(core):
528	            try:
529	                if _is_semantically_truncated(core):
530	                    _note_ff3_truncation_detected()
531	                    return True
532	            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - pure string logic; fail-OPEN + disclose degrade
533	                _note_ff3_truncation_failopen()
534	        pp_match = _DANGLING_PP_RE.search(core)
535	        if pp_match and not any(ch.isdigit() for ch in pp_match.group(1)):
536	            return True
537	    if not known_words:
538	        return False
539	    if ends_before_marker and _boundary_token_is_span_cut(
540	        _boundary_last_word(core), known_words, mode="end"
541	    ):
542	        return True
543	    if starts_after_marker:
544	        lead = _LEADING_CITATION_RE.sub("", text).lstrip()
545	        first = _boundary_first_word(lead)
546	        if first and first[:1].islower() and _boundary_token_is_span_cut(
547	            first, known_words, mode="start"
548	        ):
549	            return True
550	    return False

636	def make_question_relevance_ranker(
637	    question_text: str,
638	) -> "Callable[[str], float] | None":
639	    """Build a WEIGHT-only question-relevance ranker for the headline / Abstract render seam.
640	
641	    Returns a PURE callable ``ranker(sentence) -> float`` = the count of content words the sentence
642	    shares with ``question_text`` (higher = more on-topic). It is a RE-ORDER WEIGHT over sentences
643	    that ALREADY passed the frozen faithfulness engine (§-1.3 Principle 1 — WEIGHT, never FILTER): it
644	    can only change bullet / abstract ORDER, never drop, filter, or re-verify a claim. Reuses the
645	    EXISTING grounding tokenizer (``provenance_generator._content_words``: alphabetic, ≥3 chars,
646	    stopword-stripped) — no new model, no new magic word list, no spend. The tokenizer is imported
647	    LAZILY (mirrors ``plan_sufficiency_gate._content_words``) so this light no-spend module keeps its
648	    import-time independence from the generator stack.
649	
650	    Returns ``None`` when ``question_text`` has NO content words, so the caller threads
651	    ``sentence_relevance=None`` and gets byte-identical document order (never a degenerate all-zero
652	    ranker). PURE; no network, no GPU, no LLM."""
653	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
654	        _content_words,
655	    )
656	    question_words = _content_words(question_text or "")
657	    if not question_words:
658	        return None
659	
660	    def _ranker(sentence: str) -> float:
661	        # Overlap COUNT is a monotone on-topicness weight; it never drops or re-verifies a sentence.
662	        return float(len(_content_words(sentence or "") & question_words))
663	
664	    return _ranker

667	# I-deepfix-001 U13 (representation faithfulness): a numeric-claim headline must carry a
668	# SUBJECT/POPULATION anchor. Otherwise a bare quantity ("highest reduction of 99.4%") LEADS and
669	# misrepresents its cited span — whose subject (e.g. an animal / in-vitro model) was stripped —
670	# so a lab number reads as a human/clinical finding (strict_verify passes on decimal + content-word
671	# overlap; it does not check subject faithfulness). This DEMOTES an unanchored numeric-claim
672	# sentence BELOW subject-anchored / non-numeric verified sentences so it never occupies the lead
673	# slot. SUPPRESS / STABLE-REORDER ONLY — it never rewrites a sentence and never drops a source
674	# (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-filter): the sentence still renders if it is the only verified candidate. The
675	# frozen faithfulness engine is untouched. Default-ON; PG_KF_SUBJECT_ANCHOR=0 reverts byte-identical.
676	_U13_SUBJECT_ANCHOR_ENV = "PG_KF_SUBJECT_ANCHOR"
677	# Citation markers ("[12]") are stripped BEFORE the numeric test so a reference number is never
678	# mistaken for a numeric claim. A numeric CLAIM = a decimal, a percentage, a fold/times multiplier,
679	# any integer >=2 digits, or a small integer directly modifying a count noun (deaths/cases/...).
680	# (I-deepfix-001 U13 iter5, Codex: whole-number fold/count claims like "2-fold"/"18-fold"/"17 deaths"
681	# must be treated as numeric claims that need a subject anchor, not skipped as "non-numeric".)
682	_CITATION_STRIP_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")
683	_NUMERIC_CLAIM_RE = re.compile(
684	    r"\d\.\d"
685	    r"|\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s?%"
686	    r"|\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s?-?\s?(?:fold|times|x)\b"
687	    r"|\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s?[×✕]"  # Unicode multiplier ("2×"/"2 ×") — no trailing \b (× is non-word)
688	    r"|\b\d{2,}\b"
689	    r"|\b\d+\s+(?:deaths?|cases?|events?|fatalit\w+|patients?|participants?|subjects?|"
690	    r"infections?|hospitali\w+|admissions?|responders?)\b",
691	    re.I,
692	)
693	_SUBJECT_ANCHOR_RE = re.compile(
694	    r"\b(patients?|adults?|children|participants?|subjects?|women|men|"
695	    r"population|cohort|trial|studies|study|meta-?analysis|systematic review|"
696	    r"mice|rats?|animals?|poultry|livestock|cells?|in ?vitro|in ?vivo|humans?|"
697	    r"individuals?|cases?|infants?|neonat\w+|elderly|volunteers?|"
698	    r"carriers?|genotypes?|alleles?|variants?|homozygous|heterozygous|"
699	    r"mutations?|APOE\w*|BRCA\w*|receptors?|patients)\b",

736	def _is_subject_anchored_numeric(sentence: str) -> bool:
737	    """True iff `sentence` is safe to LEAD a headline: either it is not a numeric claim (nothing to
738	    misrepresent) OR its numeric claim is anchored to a subject/population/study noun-phrase or a
739	    capitalized proper-noun subject (drug / organism / trial name), including a sentence-initial one
740	    that is not a common function/framing word. A bare "quantity + metric" numeric fragment with no
741	    such anchor returns False and is demoted (never dropped)."""
742	    if not _NUMERIC_CLAIM_RE.search(_CITATION_STRIP_RE.sub(" ", sentence or "")):
743	        return True
744	    # ALLOWLIST-only anchoring (I-deepfix-001 U13 iter6, Codex): a numeric claim is safe to LEAD a
745	    # headline ONLY if it carries a POSITIVE subject/population term from _SUBJECT_ANCHOR_RE
746	    # (patients/adults/cohort/mice/in-vitro/carriers/APOE*/...). We do NOT try to guess a proper-noun
747	    # subject from capitalization — that repeatedly false-anchored on capitalized NON-subjects
748	    # (sentence-initial "Risk"/"Efficacy", and mid-sentence structural labels "Cycle 4"/"Dose 2"/
749	    # "Grade 3"), an open-ended blocklist that never converges. A bare quantity that names only a
750	    # drug/trial/structural label but no population is demoted (reorder-only, never dropped). This is
751	    # convergent: an allowlist cannot false-anchor on an unknown capitalized token.
752	    return bool(_SUBJECT_ANCHOR_RE.search(sentence or ""))

755	def _first_verified_sentences(
756	    verified_text: str,
757	    n: int,
758	    *,
759	    sentence_relevance: "Callable[[str], float] | None" = None,
760	    demote_unanchored: bool = False,
761	) -> list[str]:
762	    matches = [m.group(0).strip() for m in _SENTENCE_RE.finditer(verified_text or "")]
763	    # A Key Finding is a span-verified CLAIM: it must carry a citation, must NOT be
764	    # gap-disclosure boilerplate (whose 2nd sentence is cited to the gap-task sidecar, not
765	    # an evidence span), and must NOT be a markdown header line (I-perm-008 — a leaked "###"
766	    # header is never a finding). The filters together exclude every gap/header shape in a
767	    # mixed section (I-gen-006 #1178 C07/P07, Codex iter-5).
768	    # I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 2: also exclude a sentence carrying an unambiguous mid-word truncation
769	    # marker (a cut fetch span like "…comprehensi [...") so a fragment never leads a finding. Shared
770	    # by the Abstract/Conclusion harvesters; strengthening (it can only suppress a fragment).
771	    # I-wire-012 (#1326): also exclude a sentence that is render-side chrome / page-furniture
772	    # per THE ONE shared predicate (masthead/ISSN/ResearchGate/ToC/CC-license/ORCID/doc-label/
773	    # mid-word-start/incomplete) — so a chrome span never LEADS a Key-Findings / Abstract /
774	    # Conclusion / depth finding. Default-ON (PG_RENDER_CHROME_SCREEN=0 reverts to byte-identical).
775	    verified = [
776	        s for s in matches
777	        if s
778	        and not _ATX_HEADER_RE.match(s.lstrip())
779	        and _CITATION_RE.search(s)
780	        and not _GAP_MARKER_RE.search(s)
781	        and not is_truncated_fragment(s)
782	        and not _is_render_chrome_claim(s)
783	    ]
784	    # headline_relevance (I-deepfix-001): WEIGHT, not filter (§-1.3 Principle 1). When the caller
785	    # wires a question-relevance ranker, STABLE-sort the ALREADY-verified candidates by descending
786	    # on-topicness so the most on-topic verified sentence leads. It NEVER drops a sentence — the
787	    # head-``n`` slice is the pre-existing summary cap, not a faithfulness gate. None => byte-identical.
788	    # I-deepfix-001 U13: STABLE-partition subject-anchored / non-numeric verified sentences BEFORE
789	    # unanchored bare-number claims so a misrepresenting bare quantity never LEADS. When a relevance
790	    # ranker is wired the key is (anchor primary, relevance secondary); otherwise anchor-only stable
791	    # partition. Reorder-only + STABLE — an unanchored claim still appears (and still leads if it is
792	    # the ONLY candidate); nothing is dropped (§-1.3). PG_KF_SUBJECT_ANCHOR=0 => byte-identical.
793	    # I-deepfix-001 U13 iter2 (Codex P1): the anchor demote is OPT-IN via demote_unanchored so it
794	    # applies ONLY to the Key-Findings headline slot. `_first_verified_sentences` is SHARED — the
795	    # Abstract/Conclusion harvester (abstract_conclusion.py) calls it for ALL verified sentences in
796	    # DOCUMENT ORDER and picks ordered[-1] as the Conclusion; reordering there would push a demoted
797	    # bare-number sentence to the end and PROMOTE it into the Conclusion. Default False => document
798	    # order preserved for every non-KF caller (byte-identical).
799	    anchor_on = _subject_anchor_enabled() and demote_unanchored
800	    if sentence_relevance is not None:
801	        verified = sorted(
802	            verified,
803	            key=lambda s: (
804	                0 if (not anchor_on or _is_subject_anchored_numeric(s)) else 1,
805	                -_relevance_weight(sentence_relevance, s),
806	            ),
807	        )
808	    elif anchor_on:
809	        verified = sorted(
810	            verified, key=lambda s: 0 if _is_subject_anchored_numeric(s) else 1
811	        )
812	    return verified[:n]

815	def refilter_key_findings_block(report_text: str) -> str:
816	    """Drop Key-Findings bullets that became a redaction STUB after the four-role seam
817	    (I-perm-008 #1202, blueprint R7).
818	
819	    ``build_key_findings`` is assembled PRE-four-role on strict_verify-passed prose, so a lifted
820	    headline finding the four-role seam later marks non-VERIFIED is redacted in report.md into a
821	    "- **Section.** <gap stub>" pseudo-finding. The redactor runs AFTER Key Findings is built, so
822	    it cannot prevent the stub bullet; this post-redaction pass removes any KF bullet whose body
823	    now matches the gap-disclosure boilerplate (``_GAP_MARKER_RE``). With the leaked-header strip
824	    in ``build_key_findings`` each bullet is a clean single line, so a line-scoped drop is exact.
825	    If no genuine finding remains, the whole block is dropped (no empty heading). Idempotent +
826	    byte-identical when no KF bullet was redacted.
827	    """
828	    if not key_findings_enabled():
829	        return report_text
830	    header_match = re.search(r"(?m)^##\s*Key Findings\s*$", report_text)
831	    if not header_match:
832	        return report_text
833	    block_start = header_match.start()
834	    rest = report_text[header_match.end():]
835	    next_header = re.search(r"(?m)^#{1,6}\s", rest)
836	    block_end = header_match.end() + (next_header.start() if next_header else len(rest))
837	
838	    kept_lines: list[str] = []
839	    dropped_any = False
840	    for line in report_text[block_start:block_end].splitlines():
841	        # Within the bounded KF block, ANY gap-disclosure line is a redacted finding — the real
842	        # `reconcile_report_against_verdicts` replaces the WHOLE bullet (including the
843	        # "- **Section.**" prefix) with a BARE stub line, so a `- `-prefix check misses it
844	        # (Codex iter-1 P1). The block's only other lines are the heading + the italic preamble,
845	        # neither of which matches `_GAP_MARKER_RE`, so this never drops a legitimate line.
846	        if _GAP_MARKER_RE.search(line):
847	            dropped_any = True
848	            continue
849	        kept_lines.append(line)
850	    if not dropped_any:
851	        return report_text  # byte-identical when nothing was a stub
852	    new_block = "\n".join(kept_lines)
853	    if not re.search(r"(?m)^\s*-\s+\S", new_block):
854	        trimmed = report_text[:block_start] + report_text[block_end:]
855	        return re.sub(r"^\n+", "", trimmed) if block_start == 0 else trimmed
856	    if not new_block.endswith("\n"):
857	        new_block += "\n"
858	    return report_text[:block_start] + new_block + report_text[block_end:]

865	    entity_id as its title (e.g. "Generative_AI_Evidence", "Foundational_Theory"), which then leaks
866	    into a "### Generative_AI_Evidence" header / "**Generative_AI_Evidence.**" bullet. Heuristic that
867	    fires ONLY on the raw-id shape: an underscore present AND no space (a real multi-word title like

877	def _bullet_marker_integrity_ok(bullet: str) -> bool:
878	    """FIX 4(c): a Key-Findings bullet is well-formed iff — after the ``- `` list marker — it OPENS
879	    with ``**`` (the bold section-title label) AND carries a matched CLOSING ``**`` (an EVEN count of
880	    ``**`` runs). A bullet whose leading ``**`` was chopped at the render seam (the ``Job Displacement
881	    by AI Technologies.** …`` defect) opens on bare prose / a dangling ``**`` and fails here. PURE."""
882	    core = bullet.lstrip()
883	    if core.startswith("- "):
884	        core = core[2:].lstrip()
885	    if not core.startswith("**"):
886	        return False
887	    # A matched pair of bold delimiters => an EVEN number of ``**`` runs (open + close).
888	    return core.count("**") % 2 == 0
889	
890	
891	def _reemit_key_findings_bullet(title: str, sentence: str) -> str:
892	    """FIX 4(c): rebuild ONE Key-Findings bullet from its stored ``title`` + carried ``sentence`` —
893	    both already-verbatim upstream (``title`` = humanized section title; ``sentence`` = the strict_verify
894	    -PASSED span). NEVER composes new text: it re-wraps the EXACT same label + sentence in the canonical
895	    ``- **{title}.** {sentence}`` form so a bullet whose opening ``**`` was chopped is re-emitted WHOLE,
896	    never shipped mangled. When there is no title, the sentence still ships as a clean ``- {sentence}``
897	    bullet (no bold label to mismatch). PURE."""
898	    label = f"**{title}.** " if title else ""
899	    return f"- {label}{sentence}"
900	
901	
902	def build_key_findings(
903	    sections: list[Any],
904	    *,
905	    sentence_relevance: "Callable[[str], float] | None" = None,
906	) -> str:
907	    """Return a markdown "## Key Findings" block: the first verified sentence (verbatim, citation intact)
908	    from each non-dropped section with verified_text. Verified-only + extractive — never a new claim.
909	    Returns "" when disabled or when no section has verified prose (no empty heading).
910	
911	    ``sentence_relevance`` (headline_relevance, I-deepfix-001): OPTIONAL caller-wired question-
912	    relevance ranker. WEIGHT never filter (§-1.3): within-section the most on-topic verified sentence
913	    leads and bullets are GLOBALLY ordered by descending relevance; off-topic sinks past
914	    ``_MAX_BULLETS``. ``None`` (every existing caller) => byte-identical document order."""
915	    if not key_findings_enabled():
916	        return ""
917	    # FIX 4(c): carry the STORED (title, sentence) alongside the rendered bullet so a bullet whose
918	    # opening ``**`` was chopped at the render seam can be re-emitted WHOLE from the same verbatim
919	    # upstream text — never shipped mangled. (weight, rendered_bullet, title, sentence).
920	    candidates: list[tuple[float, str, str, str]] = []
921	    for sr in sections or []:
922	        if getattr(sr, "dropped_due_to_failure", False):
923	            continue
924	        # I-gen-006 (#1178) BB5-C07/P07: a 0-verified gap DISCLOSURE renders disclosure
925	        # text in verified_text (the legacy is_gap_stub or a V30 contract gap) but is NOT
926	        # span-verified prose — it must never surface as a Key-Findings "span-verified
927	        # statement". Skip every gap disclosure (universal signal: sentences_verified == 0).
928	        if getattr(sr, "is_gap_stub", False) or getattr(sr, "sentences_verified", 1) == 0:
929	            continue
930	        # I-perm-008: strip any leaked leading section header so it is never lifted as the
931	        # headline finding (a "### ..." header would otherwise break the KF block boundary).
932	        verified_text = _strip_leading_markdown_headers(getattr(sr, "verified_text", "") or "")
933	        if not verified_text.strip():
934	            continue
935	        title = humanize_section_title(getattr(sr, "title", "") or "")
936	        _marker_cap = _max_key_findings_markers()
937	        for sentence in _first_verified_sentences(
938	            verified_text,
939	            _SENTENCES_PER_SECTION,
940	            sentence_relevance=sentence_relevance,
941	            demote_unanchored=True,  # U13: KF headline is the ONLY surface that demotes bare numbers
942	        ):
943	            # Weigh the ORIGINAL sentence (all `[N]` markers intact) BEFORE capping the display run.
944	            weight = (
945	                _relevance_weight(sentence_relevance, sentence)
946	                if sentence_relevance is not None
947	                else 0.0
948	            )
949	            # I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 2: cap each adjacent citation-marker RUN to the most-relevant
950	            # few (document order). Render-only; the body + bibliography keep every reference.
951	            sentence = cap_citation_marker_runs(sentence, _marker_cap)
952	            candidates.append(
953	                (weight, _reemit_key_findings_bullet(title, sentence), title, sentence)
954	            )
955	    if sentence_relevance is not None:
956	        # STABLE descending-weight order: ties keep document order; off-topic sinks past _MAX_BULLETS.
957	        candidates = sorted(candidates, key=lambda c: -c[0])
958	    # FIX 4(c): BULLET-INTEGRITY INVARIANT — every emitted bullet must open with ``**`` and carry a
959	    # matched closing ``**``. A failing bullet is re-emitted from its STORED title + carried sentence
960	    # (both verbatim upstream), never shipped chopped. At build time the canonical form always passes;
961	    # the guard is a belt against a future edit and pairs with the render-seam whole-unit constraint.
962	    bullets: list[str] = []
963	    for _w, _rendered, _title, _sentence in candidates[:_MAX_BULLETS]:
964	        if _bullet_marker_integrity_ok(_rendered):
965	            bullets.append(_rendered)
966	        else:
967	            bullets.append(_reemit_key_findings_bullet(_title, _sentence))
968	    if not bullets:
969	        return ""
970	    # I-beatboth-011 §3.2 (#1289): HONEST self-cert label. FIX 4(d): the hedge preamble is shrunk to
971	    # ONE sentence + an appendix pointer (the full strict_verify / single-origin / not-peer-reviewed
972	    # caveats now live in the trailing audit appendix), so the report leads on findings not on a
973	    # multi-line disclaimer. LABEL honesty only — the faithfulness engine is UNTOUCHED.
974	    header = (
975	        "## Key Findings\n\n"
976	        "_Each finding is verbatim text carried up from a cited body span (strict_verify-passed); "
977	        "see the appendix for corroboration status and verification caveats._\n\n"
978	    )
979	    return header + "\n".join(bullets) + "\n\n"
980	
981	
982	# I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 6 — per-section analytical-depth layer (default-OFF, LAW VI).
983	#
984	# GENUINE grounded synthesis (NOT pattern-injection — the §-1.1 ban). For each verified section it
985	# labels the section's HEADLINE verified finding under a per-section ``**Key Findings**`` subhead and,
986	# ONLY when the section's own verified prose actually carries a challenge/limitation, lifts that
987	# verbatim challenge sentence under a ``**Challenges**`` subhead. Every emitted line is verbatim,
988	# cited, span-verified body text — so it raises the advisory analytical_depth key_findings/challenge
989	# counts HONESTLY (real content), never by injecting empty marker strings. No challenge sentence => no
990	# Challenges line (never a fabricated limitation). Default-OFF => no block => byte-identical.
991	_DEPTH_LAYER_ENV = "PG_SWEEP_DEPTH_LAYER"
992	# The SAME challenge cues the analytical_depth metric scores (kept in sync deliberately) — used to
993	# pick a REAL limitation sentence from the section's verified prose, never to fabricate one.
994	_CHALLENGE_CUE_RE = re.compile(
995	    r"\b(limitation|contradict|conflicting|gap in|insufficient evidence|notable absence|"
996	    r"remains unclear|further research|caveat|uncertain)\b",
997	    re.I,
998	)
999	# I-wire-012 (#1326) synthesis pass — a SURFACED TENSION is a verbatim verified sentence that
1000	# expresses cross-source DISAGREEMENT / opposition (however / in contrast / whereas / conversely /
1001	# disagree / diverge). Like the challenge lift it is verbatim, cited, span-verified body prose —
1002	# faithful BY IDENTITY (never a generated cross-claim recombination, which abstract_conclusion.py
1003	# proved unsound). Distinct cue set from the challenge so a section can surface BOTH.
1004	_TENSION_CUE_RE = re.compile(
1005	    r"\b(however|in contrast|by contrast|conversely|whereas|on the other hand|disagree\w*|"
1006	    r"diverg\w*|inconsistent\w*|at odds|opposite\w*)\b",
1007	    re.I,
1008	)
1009	
1010	
1011	def depth_layer_enabled() -> bool:
1012	    """DEFAULT-ON (I-deepfix-001 cov C2). Appends the per-section analytical-depth layer AND injects
1013	    the depth_synthesis DS-* live synthesizer (``make_depth_synthesizer`` ->
1014	    ``synthesize_cross_source_findings``, each within-basket cross-source finding RE-GROUNDED through
1015	    the UNCHANGED strict_verify — a finding with no grounding span is DROPPED) on the cert render path
1016	    by default, not only when the benchmark cert slate force-pins the flag.
1017	
1018	    Kill-switch (LAW VI): ``PG_SWEEP_DEPTH_LAYER=0`` (or false/off/no) => the depth layer + DS-*
1019	    synthesis are skipped and the render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the pre-cov legacy path. §-1.3: additive
1020	    keep-all analysis lever, never a cap; faithfulness untouched."""
1021	    return os.getenv(_DEPTH_LAYER_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
1022	
1023	
1024	def build_depth_layer(
1025	    sections: list[Any],
1026	    *,
1027	    synthesized_findings: list[str] | None = None,
1028	) -> str:
1029	    """Return a ``## Analytical synthesis`` block.
1030	
1031	    Two grounded layers, both verbatim/cited/span-verified — zero new unverified claims:
1032	
1033	    * ``synthesized_findings`` (I-wire-013 #1327 iter-3c): the OPTIONAL grounded digest produced by
1034	      ``depth_synthesis.synthesize_cross_source_findings`` — a list of
1035	      ``{"sentence", "tier", "label"}`` dicts (a bare string from a legacy caller is treated as
1036	      ``cross_source``). Each sentence ALREADY passed the UNCHANGED ``strict_verify`` (a synthesized
1037	      sentence with no grounding span was DROPPED) and carries the report's own ``[N]`` citations. The
1038	      per-basket TWO-TIER split renders the ``>=2``-distinct-surviving-origin findings under a
1039	      ``### Cross-source synthesis`` subhead and the post-verify COLLAPSE case (1 surviving origin)
1040	      under ``### Single-source findings`` with each bullet ``(single source)``-labeled (surfaced, not
1041	      dropped — §-1.3). ``None``/empty (the legacy call) => both subheads omitted.
1042	    * Per verified section: the headline finding under ``**Key Findings**`` and (only when the
1043	      evidence raises one) a verbatim ``**Challenges**`` / ``**Tension**`` sentence — lifted verbatim
1044	      from the section's already-verified prose.
1045	
1046	    "" when disabled, or when there is neither a synthesized finding nor any section with verified
1047	    prose (no empty heading)."""
1048	    if not depth_layer_enabled():
1049	        return ""
1050	    # I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3c — TWO-TIER render. ``synthesize_cross_source_findings`` returns dicts
1051	    # ``{"sentence", "tier", "label"}``; a bare string (a legacy caller) is treated as cross_source with
1052	    # no label. The ``>=2``-distinct-surviving-origin baskets render under ``### Cross-source synthesis``;
1053	    # the post-verify COLLAPSE case (1 surviving origin) renders under ``### Single-source findings`` with
1054	    # each bullet carrying its ``(single source)`` label — SURFACED, never dropped (§-1.3).
1055	    cross_items: list[str] = []
1056	    single_items: list[str] = []
1057	    for item in (synthesized_findings or []):
1058	        if isinstance(item, dict):
1059	            sentence = str(item.get("sentence", "") or "").strip()
1060	            tier = str(item.get("tier", "") or "cross_source").strip().lower()
1061	            label = str(item.get("label", "") or "").strip()
1062	        else:
1063	            sentence = str(item or "").strip()
1064	            tier = "cross_source"
1065	            label = ""
1066	        if not sentence:
1067	            continue
1068	        rendered = f"{sentence} {label}".strip() if label else sentence
1069	        if tier == "single_source":
1070	            single_items.append(rendered)
1071	        else:
1072	            cross_items.append(rendered)
1073	    blocks: list[str] = []
1074	    if cross_items:
1075	        # HONEST provenance sub-label (§-1.1 — a misstated provenance label is treated as lethal): the
1076	        # cross-source bullets are GENERATOR-PHRASED then re-grounded, NOT verbatim body lifts, so they
1077	        # must NOT inherit the per-section block's "verbatim … no new claim" framing. State the REAL
1078	        # guarantee: each consolidates >=2 corroborating sources and re-passed strict_verify (or was
1079	        # dropped). LABEL honesty only — the faithfulness engine is UNTOUCHED.
1080	        cross_label = (
1081	            "_Each finding below consolidates >=2 corroborating sources; it is generator-phrased "
1082	            "(not a verbatim quote) and every sentence re-passed strict_verify (span bounds + numeric "
1083	            "match + content grounding) or was dropped. Citations are the report's._"
1084	        )
1085	        blocks.append(
1086	            "### Cross-source synthesis\n\n" + cross_label + "\n\n"
1087	            + "\n".join(f"- {item}" for item in cross_items)
1088	        )
1089	    if single_items:
1090	        # HONEST sub-label for the COLLAPSE tier: drawn from a multi-source basket where only ONE source
1091	        # re-grounded after strict_verify — so it must NOT claim >=2 corroboration. Surfaced + labeled
1092	        # (§-1.3 "don't drop, label weak weak"); each sentence re-passed strict_verify or was dropped.
1093	        single_label = (
1094	            "_Each finding below is drawn from a multi-source basket where only one source re-grounded "
1095	            "after strict_verify; it is generator-phrased, marked (single source), and every sentence "
1096	            "re-passed strict_verify (span bounds + numeric match + content grounding) or was dropped. "
1097	            "Citations are the report's._"
1098	        )
1099	        blocks.append(
1100	            "### Single-source findings\n\n" + single_label + "\n\n"
1101	            + "\n".join(f"- {item}" for item in single_items)
1102	        )
1103	    _cap = _max_key_findings_markers()
1104	    # FIX-3 (#1344): the front ``## Key Findings`` block (build_key_findings) already renders
1105	    # each verified section's first sentence as a headline. When that block is rendered, the
1106	    # per-section Analytical-synthesis headline below is a CROSS-SURFACE DUPLICATE — so omit it
1107	    # here and carry only the DISTINCT Challenges/Tension sentences. Headline-LABEL de-dup only:
1108	    # nothing leaves the corpus/bibliography/body; the headline still renders once in the front
1109	    # KF block and once in the section body. Built only when the front KF block is rendered.
1110	    front_headlines: set[str] = set()
1111	    if key_findings_enabled():
1112	        for sr in sections or []:
1113	            if getattr(sr, "dropped_due_to_failure", False):
1114	                continue
1115	            if getattr(sr, "is_gap_stub", False) or getattr(sr, "sentences_verified", 1) == 0:
1116	                continue
1117	            _vt = _strip_leading_markdown_headers(getattr(sr, "verified_text", "") or "")
1118	            _first = _first_verified_sentences(_vt, 1)
1119	            if _first:
1120	                front_headlines.add(_first[0])
1121	    for sr in sections or []:
1122	        if getattr(sr, "dropped_due_to_failure", False):
1123	            continue
1124	        if getattr(sr, "is_gap_stub", False) or getattr(sr, "sentences_verified", 1) == 0:
1125	            continue
1126	        verified_text = _strip_leading_markdown_headers(getattr(sr, "verified_text", "") or "")
1127	        if not verified_text.strip():
1128	            continue
1129	        ordered = _first_verified_sentences(verified_text, 10_000)
1130	        if not ordered:
1131	            continue
1132	        title = humanize_section_title(getattr(sr, "title", "") or "Section") or "Section"
1133	        headline = cap_citation_marker_runs(ordered[0], _cap)
1134	        lines = [f"### {title}", ""]
1135	        # Lift a REAL challenge sentence (a verbatim verified sentence carrying a challenge cue) —
1136	        # never fabricate one. Prefer one distinct from the headline.
1137	        challenge = next(
1138	            (s for s in ordered if _CHALLENGE_CUE_RE.search(s) and s != headline),
1139	            "",
1140	        )
1141	        if challenge:
1142	            lines.append(f"**Challenges** {cap_citation_marker_runs(challenge, _cap)}")
1143	        # I-wire-012 (#1326): surface a REAL cross-source tension — a verbatim verified sentence
1144	        # carrying a disagreement/opposition cue, distinct from the headline AND the challenge. Never
1145	        # fabricated: if the section's own verified prose raises no opposition, no Tension line.
1146	        # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) STEP 4 anti-signal: also exclude a Tension that is the SAME sentence as the
1147	        # headline (or challenge) modulo citation markers / whitespace — the exact ``!= headline`` string
1148	        # guard misses a formatting-only difference, which is how a Tension line duplicated its own headline
1149	        # in the drb_72 report. Normalized = strip [N] markers + collapse whitespace + lowercase. Default-ON
1150	        # kill-switch PG_TENSION_HEADLINE_DEDUP (OFF => byte-identical exact-string guard only).
1151	        _tension_dedup_on = os.getenv("PG_TENSION_HEADLINE_DEDUP", "1").strip().lower() not in (
1152	            "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
1153	        )
1154	
1155	        def _norm_dedup(_s: str) -> str:
1156	            return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", re.sub(r"\[[^\]]*\]", "", _s or "")).strip().lower()
1157	
1158	        _hl_norm = _norm_dedup(headline) if _tension_dedup_on else None
1159	        _ch_norm = _norm_dedup(challenge) if _tension_dedup_on else None
1160	        tension = next(
1161	            (s for s in ordered
1162	             if _TENSION_CUE_RE.search(s) and s != headline and s != challenge
1163	             and (not _tension_dedup_on or (_norm_dedup(s) != _hl_norm and _norm_dedup(s) != _ch_norm))),
1164	            "",
1165	        )
1166	        if tension:
1167	            lines.append(f"**Tension** {cap_citation_marker_runs(tension, _cap)}")
1168	        # FIX-3 (#1344): emit the headline line ONLY when there is no distinct Challenges/Tension
1169	        # to carry this subsection. If the front KF block already owns the headline, omit the whole
1170	        # subsection (no duplicate); if the KF block is off, the headline has no other home so keep it.
1171	        if not challenge and not tension:
1172	            if ordered[0] in front_headlines:
1173	                continue  # KF block already owns the headline -> omit, don't duplicate
1174	            lines.append(f"**Key Findings** {headline}")  # KF block off -> headline's only home
1175	        if len(lines) == 2:  # only "### title" + "" -> no distinct content to carry
1176	            continue
1177	        blocks.append("\n".join(lines))
1178	    if not blocks:
1179	        return ""
1180	    header = (
1181	        "## Analytical synthesis\n\n"
1182	        "_Per-section, the distinct **Challenges**/**Tension** the evidence itself raises (the "
1183	        "headline finding lives in the Key Findings block above and is not repeated here); a "
1184	        "headline appears below only when the section raises no separate tension/challenge. All "
1185	        "carried up verbatim from cited, span-verified body prose; no new claim._\n\n"
1186	    )
1187	    return header + "\n\n".join(blocks) + "\n\n"
